text: If Configurations of the Sun, Moon, and stars, then Produce a multiplicity of changes
If Conjunction of the Moon, then Diminishes the power of the Sun
If First quarter of the Moon, then Diminishes the power of the Sun
If Last quarter of the Moon, then Diminishes the power of the Sun
If Opposition of the Moon, then Diminishes the power of the Sun
If Certain aspects of the Moon towards the Sun, then Tempests and gales of wind
If Certain aspects of the fixed stars towards the Sun, then Tempests and gales of wind
If certain aspects of the Moon, or the fixed stars, towards the Sun, then tempests and gales of wind

If visible position or configuration of the stars and the Moon towards the Sun, then predict the peculiar constitution of the atmosphere in every season, as, for instance, with regard to its greater heat or moisture, or other similar qualities
If constitution of the Ambient, then destiny and disposition of every human being
If symptoms in the Ambient, then events as happen to men by the influence of the Ambient
If Events which necessarily and fully happen, whether exciting fear or creating joy, arriving unforeseen, then will either overwhelm the mind with terror or destroy its composure by sudden delight

If Events which necessarily and fully happen, whether exciting fear or creating joy, are foreknown, then the mind will have been previously prepared for their reception, and will preserve an equable calmness
If Existing agency is manifold and great, and of a power impossible to be resisted, then the corresponding event which it indicates shall absolutely take place
If Exciting causes are counteracted by some interposing influence, then another event shall not happen
If The weather and the seasons, and the indications of the fixed stars, as well as the configurations of the Moon, then afford means of prognostication
If Stars in the head of Aries, then Influence similar to Mars and Saturn
If Stars in the mouth of Aries, then Influence similar to Mercury and Saturn
If Stars in the hinder foot of Aries, then Influence similar to Mars
If Stars in the tail of Aries, then Influence similar to Venus
If Stars in Taurus, in the abscission of the sign, then Temperament like Venus and Saturn
If Stars in the Pleiades, then Like the Moon and Mars
If Bright and ruddy star of the Hyades called Facula, then Same temperament as Mars
If Other stars of the Hyades, then Resemble Saturn and partly Mercury
If Stars at the top of the horns of Taurus, then Like Mars
If Stars in the feet of Gemini, then Influence similar to Mercury and moderately to Venus
If Bright stars in the thighs of Gemini, then Like Saturn
If Bright star on the head of Gemini, which precedes and is called Apollo, then Like Mercury
If Bright star on the head of Gemini, which follows, called Hercules, then Like Mars
If Stars in the eyes of Cancer, then Same influence as Mercury and moderately like Mars
If Stars in the claws of Cancer, then Like Saturn and Mercury
If Nebulous mass in the breast of Cancer, called the Praesepe, then Same efficacy as Mars and the Moon
If Stars placed on either side of the nebulous mass of Cancer, called the Asini, then Influence similar to that of Mars and the Sun
If Two stars in the head of Leo, then Like Saturn and partly like Mars
If Three stars in the neck of Leo, then Like Saturn and in some degree like Mercury
If Bright star in the heart of Leo, called Regulus, then Agrees with Mars and Jupiter
If Stars in the loins of Leo, then Like Saturn and Venus
If Bright star in the tail of Leo, then Like Saturn and Venus
If Stars in the thighs of Leo, then Resemble Venus and in some degree Mercury
If Stars in the head of Virgo, then Operate like Mercury and somewhat like Mars
If Star at the top of the southern wing of Virgo, then Operate like Mercury and somewhat like Mars
If Other bright stars in the southern wing of Virgo, then Resemble Mercury in their influence and also Venus moderately
If Stars about the girdle of Virgo, then Resemble Mercury in their influence and also Venus moderately
If Bright star in the northern wing of Virgo, called Vindemiator, then Same influence as Saturn and Mercury
If Star called Spica Virginis, then Like Venus and partly Mars
If Stars at the points of the feet of Virgo, then Like Mercury and also Mars, moderately
If Stars at the bottom of the garments of Virgo, then Like Mercury and also Mars, moderately
If Stars at the points of the claws of Scorpio, then Operate like Jupiter and Mercury
If Stars in the middle of the claws of Scorpio, then Like Saturn and in some degree like Mars
If Bright stars in the front of the body of Scorpio, then Effect similar to Mars and partly to Saturn
If Three stars in the body of Scorpio, the middle one of which, called Antares, is ruddy and more luminous, then Similar to Mars and moderately to Jupiter
If Stars in the joints of the tail of Scorpio, then Like Saturn and partly like Venus
If Stars in the sting of Scorpio, then Like Mercury and Mars
If Nebula in Scorpio, then Like Mars and the Moon
If Stars at the point of the arrow in Sagittarius, then Influence similar to that of Mars and the Moon
If Stars on the bow of Sagittarius, then Act like Jupiter and Mars
If Stars at the grasp of the hand of Sagittarius, then Act like Jupiter and Mars
If Nebula in the face of Sagittarius, then Like the Sun and Mars
If Ares is ruddy and more luminous, then Similar to Mars and moderately to Jupiter
If Stars in the joints of the tail, then Like Saturn and partly like Venus
If Stars in the sting, then Like Mercury and Mars
If Nebula, then Like Mars and the Moon
If Stars on the bow in Sagittarius, then Act like Jupiter and Mars
If Stars at the grasp of the hand in Sagittarius, then Act like Jupiter and Mars
If Nebula in the face in Sagittarius, then Like the Sun and Mars
If Stars in the waist in Sagittarius, then Resemble Jupiter, and also Mercury moderately
If Stars in the back in Sagittarius, then Resemble Jupiter, and also Mercury moderately
If Stars in the feet in Sagittarius, then Jupiter and Saturn
If Four-sided figure in the tail in Sagittarius, then Similar to Venus, and in some degree to Saturn
If Stars in the horns of Capricorn, then Efficacy similar to that of Venus, and partly to that of Mars
If Stars in the mouth of Capricorn, then Like Saturn, and partly like Venus
If Stars in the feet of Capricorn, then Act in the same manner as Mars and Mercury
If Stars in the belly of Capricorn, then Act in the same manner as Mars and Mercury
If Stars in the tail of Capricorn, then Like Saturn and Jupiter
If Stars in the shoulders of Aquarius, then Operate like Saturn and Mercury
If Stars in the left hand of Aquarius, then Operate like Saturn and Mercury
If Stars in the face of Aquarius, then Operate like Saturn and Mercury
If Stars in the thighs of Aquarius, then Influence more consonant with that of Mercury, and in a less degree with that of Saturn
If Stars in the stream of water in Aquarius, then Power similar to that of Saturn, and, moderately, to that of Jupiter
If Stars in Pisces, which are in the head of the southern fish, then Same influence as Mercury, and, in some degree, as Saturn
If Stars in the body of the southern fish in Pisces, then Like Jupiter and Mercury
If Stars in the tail of the southern fish in Pisces, then Like Saturn, and, moderately, like Mercury
If Stars in the southern line of the southern fish in Pisces, then Like Saturn, and, moderately, like Mercury
If Stars on the body of the northern fish in Pisces, then Resemble Jupiter, and also Venus in some degree
If Stars on the back-bone of the northern fish in Pisces, then Resemble Jupiter, and also Venus in some degree
If Stars in the northern line of the northern fish in Pisces, then Like Saturn and Jupiter
If Bright star in the knot of the northern fish in Pisces, then Acts like Mars, and moderately like Mercury
If Antares, then Partakes of the nature of Mars
If Bright star in the mouth of Piscis Australis, then Same influence as Venus and Mercury
If Cetus, then Like Saturn
If Stars on the shoulders of Orion, then Operate similarly to Mars and Mercury
If Other bright stars of Orion, then Like Jupiter and Saturn
If Last bright star of Fluvius (or Eridanus), then Same influence as Jupiter
If Rest of Fluvius (or Eridanus), then Like Saturn
If Lepus, then Like Saturn and Mercury
If Bright star in the mouth of Canis, then Like Jupiter, and partly like Mars
If Other stars of Canis, then Like Venus
If Bright star of Procyon, then Like Mercury, and in some degree like Mars
If Bright stars of Hydrus, then Like Saturn and Venus
If Crater, then Like Venus, and in some degree like Mercury
If Corvus, then Like Mars and Saturn
If Bright stars of Argo, then Like Saturn and Jupiter
If Stars in the human part of Centaurus, then Same influence as Venus and Mercury
If Bright stars in the horse’s part of Centaurus, then Like Venus and Jupiter
If Bright stars of Lupus, then Like Saturn, and partly like Mars
If Ara, then Like Venus, and also Mercury in some degree
If Bright stars of Corona Australis, then Like Saturn and Jupiter
If Fixed stars of the greatest magnitude, then Most efficacious
If Fixed stars in, or near, the ecliptic, then More powerful than those more remote from it
If Fixed stars with north latitude and declination, then Affect us most
If Fixed stars in the zenith, then Influence more than others, more remote
If Fixed stars in partial conjunction with any planets, then Increase of power
If Fixed stars in the antiscions of any planets, then Increase of power
If Fixed stars which rise and set with any planet, then Increase of power
If Fixed stars which culminate with any planet, then Increase of power
If Fixed stars beheld by any planet, then Increase of power
If Sun in the eastern point (ascendant), then Damps occasioned by the night begin to be dried up
If Winds blowing from the east, then Arid and free from moisture
If Sun in the southern point (mid-heaven), then Greater warmth and heat
If Winds blowing from the south, then Hot and rarefying
If Sun in the western point (occidental angle), then Moisture, which had been overpowered during the day, recommences its operation
If Winds proceeding from the west, then Light and damp
If Sun in the northern point (angle of the lower heaven), then Far removed from it in declination
If Winds proceeding from the north, then Cold and frosty
If Stars effecting heat, then Operate more vigorously in constitutions of heat
If Stars effecting moisture, then Operate more vigorously in constitutions of moisture
If Stars effecting heat attempered by constitutions of cold, then Less efficacious
If Stars producing moisture attempered by constitutions of dryness, then Less efficacious
If Sun in Cancer, then Summer
If Sun in Capricorn, then Winter
If Sun in Aries, then Day and night equal
If Sun in Libra, then Day and night equal
If Signs are masculine, then Diurnal
If Signs are feminine, then Nocturnal
If Trine aspect, then Harmonious
If Sextile aspect, then Harmonious
If Opposition aspect, then Discordant
If Quartile aspect, then Discordant
If Sun in summer semicircle, then Day longer than night
If Sun in winter semicircle, then Night longer than day
If Parts distant from each other in the space of one sign only, then inconjunct
If Parts distant from each other in the space of five signs, then inconjunct
If Leo, then house for the Sun
If Cancer, then house for the Moon
If Saturn is cold and inimical to heat, moving also in a superior orbit most remote from the luminaries, then Saturn occupies the signs opposite to Cancer and Leo: these are Aquarius and Capricorn
If Jupiter has a favourable temperament, and is situated beneath the sphere of Saturn, then Jupiter occupies the next two signs, Sagittarius and Pisces
If Mars is dry in nature, and beneath the sphere of Jupiter, then Mars takes the next two signs, of a nature similar to his own, viz. Aries and Scorpio
If Venus, possessing a favourable temperament, and placed beneath the sphere of Mars, then Venus takes the next two signs, Taurus and Libra

If Mercury never has greater distance from the Sun than the space of one sign, and is beneath all the other planets, then Mercury is placed nearest to both luminaries, and the remaining two signs, Gemini and Virgo, are allotted to him
If Saturn’s day house, then Aquarius
If Saturn’s night house, then Capricorn
If Jupiter’s day house, then Sagittarius
If Jupiter’s night house, then Pisces
If Mars’ day house, then Aries
If Mars’ night house, then Scorpio
If Venus’ day house, then Taurus
If Venus’ night house, then Libra
If Mercury’s day house, then Gemini
If Mercury’s night house, then Virgo
If Sun in Aries, then Sun begins to lengthen the days, and the influence of his heating nature increases
If Sun in Libra, then Sun enters into the more southern and lower semicircle
If Saturn in Libra, then Saturn obtains his exaltation
If Saturn in Aries, then Saturn's fall
If Moon after conjunction with the Sun in Aries, then Moon makes her first appearance, and begins to augment her light in Taurus
If Moon in Taurus, then Moon's exaltation
If Jupiter in Cancer, then Jupiter becomes most northerly, and augments his peculiar influence
If Jupiter in Cancer, then Jupiter's exaltation
If Jupiter in Capricorn, then Jupiter's fall
If Mars in Capricorn, then Mars possesses a fiery nature, which receives its greatest intensity
If Mars in Capricorn, then Mars becomes most southerly
If Mars in Capricorn, then Mars' exaltation
If Mars in Cancer, then Mars' fall
If Venus in Pisces, then Venus is of a moist nature, and becomes chiefly moist
If Venus in Pisces, then A dampness begins to be perceptible in the atmosphere
If Venus in Pisces, then Venus derives an augmentation of her own proper influence
If Venus in Pisces, then Venus' exaltation
If Venus in Virgo, then Venus' fall
If Mercury in Virgo, then Mercury's exaltation
If Mercury in Virgo, then The autumnal dryness makes its first appearance
If Mercury in Pisces, then Mercury's fall
If A planet possesses two rights of dominion in one and the same sign, then Such planet is universally placed first in order in that sign.
If A malefic does not possess two rights of dominion in the sign, then It is always placed last.
If Lord of the exaltation, then Placed first.
If Lord of the triplicity, then Placed after the lord of the exaltation.
If Lord of the house, then Placed after the lord of the triplicity.
If Malefics in Cancer or Leo, then Malefics occupy the first degrees.
If Mars in Cancer, then Mars receives the first degrees.
If Saturn in Leo, then Saturn receives the first degrees.
If Venus obtains the sole government of Taurus or Pisces, then Venus receives in addition one degree.

If Planet holds familiarity with the place which it actually occupies by two, or more, of the prescribed modes of connection, then Its influence and energy are specially augmented by the familiarity it thus holds with the sign which encompasses it, and which is similar in influence and co-operates with it.
If A planet occupies a place adverse and dissimilar in condition to itself, then Much of its influence is dissipated and lost.
If Distances between planets or luminaries are but trifling, then The planet which precedes is said to apply to that which follows.
If Distances between planets or luminaries are but trifling, then That which follows to be separating from that which precedes.

If Nations under southern parallels (between the equinoctial line and the summer tropic) have the Sun in their zenith, then Black complexion, thick and curled hair, ugly in person, contracted stature, hot disposition, and fierce manners
If Nations under more remote northern parallels (under the Arctic circle and beyond), then Fair complexion, straight hair, large bodies and full stature, cold in disposition, and wild in manners
If Nations between the summer tropic and the Arctic circle have a well-tempered atmosphere, then Proportionate stature and complexion, good natural disposition, live in societies, and are civilised
If Nations verging towards the south, then More industrious and ingenious, and more adapted to the sciences
If Vicinity of the zodiac to their zenith, then Gives activity and an intellectual impulse to their minds
If Natives of countries towards the east, then Excel in courage, acting boldly and openly
If Western parts are lunar, then Inhabitants of the west are milder, more effeminate and reserved
If North-western parts of the first quadrant (Europe) are in familiarity with the northwest triplicity (Aries, Leo, and Sagittarius), then Governed by Jupiter and Mars
If Britain, Galatia, Germany, and Barsania have a greater share of familiarity with Aries and Mars, then Inhabitants are wilder, bolder, and more ferocious
If Italy, Apulia, Sicily, and Gaul are in familiarity with Leo and the Sun, then Inhabitants are more imperious, yet kind and benevolent, and careful of the commonwealth
If Tuscany, Celtica, and Spain, are connected with Sagittarius and Jupiter, then Inhabitants are lovers of freedom, simplicity, and elegance

If South-eastern parts of the first quadrant (Thrace, Macedonia, Illyria, Hellas, Achaia, and Crete, as well as the Cyclad Isles and the shores of Asia Minor and of Cyprus) assume a connection with the south-east triplicity (Taurus, Virgo, and Capricorn), then Ruled by Venus and Saturn; Mercury also has a proportionate dominion over them
If Inhabitants are subjected to the rulers of both triplicities (Mars, Jupiter, Venus, Mercury), then Enjoy a favourable temperament of mind and of body

If Cyclad Isles, and of the shores of Asia Minor and of Cyprus, are more particularly under the influence of Taurus and Venus, then Voluptuous, fond of elegance, and over-studious in their attention to the body
If People of Hellas, Achaia, and Crete, have a stronger familiarity with Virgo and Mercury, then Learned and scientific, preferring the cultivation of the mind to the care of the body
If People of Macedonia, Thrace, and Illyria, are chiefly influenced by Capricorn and Saturn, then Greedy of wealth, inferior in civilization, and have no ordinances of civil polity

If Parts of Asia Magna contained in India, Arriana, Gedrosia, Parthia, Media, Persia, Babylonia, Mesopotamia, and Assyria, are situated in the south-east of the whole earth, and have due familiarity with the southeast triplicity (Taurus, Virgo, and Capricorn), then Governed by Venus, Mercury, and Saturn

If Parthia, Media, and Persia, have a more particular familiarity with Taurus and Venus, then Dwellers in those countries wear splendid garments, and clothe the whole person entirely, except the breast; they are also fond of elegance and refinement

If Countries about Babylon, Mesopotamia, and Assyria, are connected with Virgo and Mercury, then Inhabitants are consequently studious of the sciences, and, among other attainments, excel in making observations on the five planets
If India, Arriana, and Gedrosia, are connected with Capricorn and Saturn, then Natives of these regions are, therefore, ill-formed in person, of dirty habits, and barbarous manners

If Idumza, Ceelesyria, Judza, Phoenicia, Chaldza, Orchynia, and Arabia Felix, occupy a situation in the vicinity of the middle of the earth, and in the north-west of the quadrant to which they actually belong: hence they are in familiarity with the north-west triplicity (Aries, Leo, and Sagittarius), then Ruled by Jupiter and Mars, together with Mercury
If Inhabitants of Ccelesyria, [dumza, and Judza, are principally influenced by Aries and Mars, then Generally audacious, atheistical
If Aries, Leo, and Sagittarius are prominent, then Natives are skilful in trade and all mercantile affairs, heedless of danger, yet treacherous, servile, and thoroughly fickle.
If Aries and Mars are prominent, then Inhabitants are audacious and atheistical.
If Leo and the Sun are prominent, then Inhabitants are more simple and humane in disposition, studious of astrology, and pay greater reverence than all other nations to the Sun.

If Sagittarius and Jupiter are prominent, then The country is fertile, and abundantly productive of spices, and its inhabitants are well-proportioned in person, free in all their habits of life, and liberal in all their contracts and dealings.

If Gemini, Libra, and Aquarius are prominent and Saturn and Jupiter are in matutine positions, then Inhabitants worship Jupiter and the Sun, are abundantly rich in all things, possess much gold, and are dainty and luxurious in their diet. They are also learned in theology, skilled in magic, just in all their dealings, free and noble-minded, holding dishonesty and wickedness in abhorrence, strongly imbued with the softer affections of nature; and, in a worthy cause, they will even readily embrace death to preserve their friends. They are, furthermore, chaste in marriage, elegant and splendid in their dress, charitable and beneficent, and of enlightened intellect.
If Gemini and Mercury are prominent, then Inhabitants are more acute in apprehension, but less tenacious of their probity.
If Libra and Venus are prominent, then Natives are endowed with much wealth and many luxuries, and take delight in poetry and songs.
If Aquarius and Saturn are prominent, then Inhabitants are less polished in manners, and more austere and uncouth.

If Cancer, Scorpio, and Pisces are prominent and Mars and Venus are in matutine positions, then People are servile in mind, diligent in labour, yet fraudulent, knavish, and thievish; they enter into foreign armies for the sake of hire, and make prisoners and slaves of their own countrymen: besides which, they are continually subject to intestine broils.

If Mars is exalted in Capricorn and Venus is exalted in Pisces, then Women have strong attachments and kindly affections to their husbands, are vigilant and careful in domestic affairs, and highly industrious: they also act as servants, and labour for the men, with all due obedience, in every thing.

If Cancer and the Moon are prominent, then Male population is, generally speaking, slavish in its habits, timid and superstitious, while the greater part of the women, owing to the matutine and masculine position of the Moon, are of masculine manners, ambitious of command, and warlike.
If Scorpio and Mars are prominent, then Inhabitants are bold, wicked, treacherous, and laborious.
If Pisces and Jupiter are prominent, then Inhabitants are wealthy, of mercantile habits, living in freedom and in community, faithful to their engagements, and honest in their dealings.

If Cancer, Scorpio, and Pisces are prominent and Mars and Venus are in vespertine position, then Dwellers are doubly governed by a man and a woman, who are both children of the same mother ; the man rules the males, and the woman the females. They are extremely hot in constitution, and desirous of women; their marriages are usually made by violence, and in many districts the local princes first enjoy the brides of their subjects : in some places, however, the women are common to all. The influence of Venus causes the whole people to delight in personal ornaments, and in being arrayed in female attire : nevertheless, that of Mars renders them courageous, crafty, addicted to magic, and fearless of dangers.
If Cancer and the Moon are prominent, then Inhabitants live in community, attend to mercantile pursuits, and enjoy abundantly all the blessings of nature.

If Scorpio and Mars are prominent, then Natives are ferocious and pugnacious in the highest degree; eaters of human flesh, utterly indifferent to danger, and so regardless and prodigal of blood, as to slay each other without hesitation on the slightest cause.

If Pisces and Jupiter are prominent, then People are frank and simple in manners, fond of employment, well disposed, fond of the decencies of life, and, for the most part, free and unrestrained in their actions: they worship Jupiter by the name of Ammon.

If Gemini, Libra, and Aquarius are prominent and Saturn, Jupiter and Mercury are in vespertine figuration, then Inhabitants cherish due love and reverence for the gods, and dedicate themselves to their service. They are addicted to sepulchral ceremonies.

If Planets in vespertine figuration in Gemini, Libra, and Aquarius, governed by Saturn, Jupiter, and Mercury, then Inhabitants cherish love and reverence for the gods and dedicate themselves to their service
If Planets in vespertine figuration in Gemini, Libra, and Aquarius, governed by Saturn, Jupiter, and Mercury, then Inhabitants are addicted to sepulchral ceremonies
If Malefics in figuration with Venus in vespertine position, then Men are effeminate and debased in mind

If Gemini and Mercury influence, then Natives are highly intellectual and sensible, and gifted with capacity for every undertaking, above all, for the attainment of wisdom, and an insight into divine mysteries
If Gemini and Mercury influence, then Natives are magicians, performing secret rites and ceremonies
If Gemini and Mercury influence, then Natives are calculated for the prosecution of all scientific inquiry
If Libra and Venus influence, then Natives are of warmer constitution and more hasty disposition
If Libra and Venus influence, then Natives enjoy life in all its plentitude and abundance
If Aquarius and Saturn influence, then Natives feed on flesh and fish indiscriminately
If Eclipse in a zodiac place, then Countries in familiarity with that place will be influenced
If Saturn, Jupiter, and Mars are stationary, then Portentous indications
If Ecliptical place near the eastern horizon, then Effect will begin to be manifested in the course of the first four months after the date of the eclipse
If Ecliptical place near the eastern horizon, then General height, or intensity, will take place in, or about, the first third part of the whole extent of its duration
If Ecliptical place in or near the mid-heaven, then Effect will begin to appear in the second four months
If Ecliptical place in or near the mid-heaven, then General intensity will occur about the second third part
If Ecliptical place near the western horizon, then Effect will begin in the third four months
If Ecliptical place near the western horizon, then General intensity in the last third part of its whole duration
If eclipse place and fixed stars/planets in dominion are in signs of human shape, then effect will fall upon the human race
If eclipse place and fixed stars/planets in dominion are in terrestrial or quadrupedal signs, then event would be indicated to happen to animals of similar form
If eclipse place and fixed stars/planets in dominion are in signs shaped like reptiles, then serpents and creatures of that description will be affected
If eclipse place and fixed stars/planets in dominion are in signs bearing the figure of ferocious beasts, then event will affect savage and destructive animals
If eclipse place and fixed stars/planets in dominion are in signs figured like tame beasts, then it will operate on animals serviceable to mankind, and of domestic character
If terrestrial signs situated in the north, about the Arctic circle, then indicate sudden earthquakes
If terrestrial signs situated in the south, then sudden deluges of rain
If ruling places are situated in signs shaped like winged animals, then event will take effect on birds
If ruling places are situated in signs shaped like winged animals, then will chiefly attach to those which afford food to man
If ruling places are situated in signs formed like creatures which swim, and in marine signs, then effect will be felt by marine animals, and in the navigation of fleets
If ruling places are in river signs, then it will attach to animals living in rivers and in fresh waters
If ruling places are in Argo, then both sea and freshwater animals will be affected by it
If ruling places are situated in tropical or in equinoctial signs, then they presignify changes in the state of the atmosphere, at the respective season to which each sign is appropriated
If ruling places should be in the sign of the vernal equinox, then they will produce an effect on the buds of the vine and fig, and of such other trees as sprout forth at that season
If ruling places should be in the sign of the summer tropic, then the event will affect the gathering and depositing of fruits
If ruling places should be in the sign of the summer tropic, then it will impede the rising of the Nile
If ruling places should be in the sign of the autumnal equinox, then it will operate on grain and on various sorts of herbs
If ruling places should be in the sign of the winter tropic, then on potherbs, esculent vegetables, and such birds and fishes as arrive in that season
If equinoctial signs, then indicate the circumstances liable to happen in ecclesiastical concerns, and in religious matters
If tropical signs, then give warning of changes in the atmosphere and in political affairs
If fixed signs, then of changes in institutions and in buildings
If bicorporeal signs, then future event will fall alike on princes and their subjects
If ruling places situated in the east, during the time of the eclipse, then fruits and seeds, incipient institutions, and the age of youth, will be affected
If ruling places in the mid-heaven above the earth, then coming event will relate to ecclesiastical affairs, to kings and princes, and to the middle age
If ruling places in the west, then it will influence the laws, old age, and persons about to die
If operative stars in vespertine position to a solar eclipse, then said stars will most usually much diminish the effect
If operative stars in matutine position to a lunar eclipse, then said stars will most usually much diminish the effect
If operative stars in opposition, then render it moderate
If operative stars in matutine position to a solar eclipse, then they greatly augment and extend it
If operative stars in vespertine to a lunar eclipse, then they greatly augment and extend it
If Saturn may be sole governor, then produce disasters concomitant with cold

If Saturn may be sole governor, then induce among men lingering diseases, consumptions, declines, rheumatisms, disorders from watery humours, and attacks of the quartan ἀρὰ ; as well as exile, poverty, and a general mass of evils, griefs, and alarms : deaths also will be frequent, but chiefly among persons advanced inage
If Jupiter should be lord alone, then thoroughly improve and benefit all things
If Jupiter should be lord alone, then promote honour, happiness, content, and peace, by augmenting all the necessaries and comforts of life, and all mental and bodily advantages
If Jupiter should be lord alone, then induces also favours, benefits, and gifts emanating from royalty, and adds greater lustre to kings themselves, increasing their dignity and magnanimity
If Mars, when governing alone, then generally causes such mischief and destruction as are concomitant with dryness

If Mars, when governing alone, then foreign wars will be excited, accompanied with intestine divisions, captivity, slaughter, insurrections of the people, and wrath of princes against their subjects; together with sudden and untimely death, the consequence of these disturbances

If Mars, when governing alone, then Feverish disorders, tertian agues, and hemorrhages will take place, and will be rapidly followed by painful death, carrying off chiefly youthful persons: and conflagration, murder, impiety, every infraction of the law, adultery, rape, robbery, -and all kinds of violence will be practised
If Venus, alone in domination, then generally produces the same effects as Jupiter, yet with greater suavity and more agreeably

If Venus, alone in domination, then Glory, honour, and joy will attend mankind ; happy marriages will be contracted, and the fortunate pairs will be blest with numerous children. Every undertaking will proceed prosperously, wealth will increase, and the conduct of human life will be altogether pure, simple and pious ; due reverence being paid to all holy and sacred institutions, and harmony subsisting between princes and their subjects
If Mercury, if possessing dominion, then usually conjoined with one or other of the planets beforementioned, and is conformed and assimilated to their natures
If Mercury, if possessing dominion, then promote industry and skill in business; but, at the same time, thievish propensities, robberies, and plots of treachery
If Mercury configurated with the malefics, then produce calamities in navigation, and will also cause dry and parching diseases, quotidian fever, cough, consumption, and hemorrhage
If Mercury in vespertine position, then a diminution of waters
If Mercury in matutine position, then an augmentation of waters
If Eclipses with black or greenish colors, then Portend effects similar to those produced by Saturn's nature
If Eclipses with white colors, then Portend effects similar to those operated by Jupiter
If Eclipses with reddish colors, then Portend effects similar to those by Mars
If Eclipses with yellow colors, then Portend effects similar to those by Venus
If Eclipses with various colors, then Portend effects similar to those by Mercury
If Comets, then Operate effects like those of Mars and Mercury; exciting wars, heated and turbulent dispositions in the atmosphere, and in the constitutions of men, with all their evil consequences
If New Moon nearest to the vernal equinox, then New Moon of the Year
If New or full Moon taking place when the Sun is nearest to the first point of Aries, then Spring should be dated
If New or full Moon taking place when the Sun is nearest to the first point of Cancer, then Summer should be dated
If New or full Moon taking place when the Sun is nearest to the first point of Libra, then Autumn should be dated
If New or full Moon taking place when the Sun is nearest to the first point of Capricorn, then Winter should be dated

If Front parts of Aries, then Excite rain and wind
If Middle parts of Aries, then Temperate
If Back parts of Aries, then Heating and pestilential
If Northern parts of Aries, then Heating and pernicious
If Southern parts of Aries, then Cooling and frosty
If Taurus, then Chiefly warm
If Front parts of Taurus, especially those near the Pleiades, then Produce earthquakes
If The front parts of signs, then excite rain and wind
If The middle parts of signs, then are temperate
If The parts behind signs, then are heating and pestilential

If The southern parts of signs, then cooling and frosty
If The front parts of Taurus, especially those near the Pleiades, then produce earthquakes, clouds and winds
If The middle parts of Taurus, then are moistening and cooling
If The parts behind Taurus, and near the Hyades, then are fiery, and cause meteors and lightnings
If The northern parts of Taurus, then are temperate
If The southern parts of Taurus, then are turbulent and variable
If The leading parts of Gemini, then produce mischief by moisture
If The middle parts of Gemini, then are entirely temperate
If The latter parts of Gemini, then mixed and turbulent

If The southern parts of Gemini, then are dry and heating
If The anterior part of Cancer near the Presepe, then are oppressively hot and suffocating
If The middle parts of Cancer, then are temperate
If The latter parts of Cancer, then excite wind
If Both its northern and southern parts of Cancer, then are equally fiery and scorching
If The anterior parts of Leo, then are oppressively and pestilentially hot
If The middle parts of Leo, then are temperate
If The parts behind Leo, then are injurious by means of moisture
If The northern parts of Leo, then produce variation and heat
If The southern parts of Leo, then produce moisture
If The front parts of Virgo, then are chiefly warm and noxious
If The middle parts of Virgo, then are temperate
If The latter parts of Virgo, then are watery
If The northern parts of Virgo, then promote wind
If The southern parts of Virgo, then are temperate

If Its hinder parts of Libra, then are watery
If The northern parts of Libra, then cause variable winds
If The southern parts of Libra, then are moistening and pestilential

If The middle parts of Scorpio, then are temperate
If The latter parts of Scorpio, then excite earthquakes

If Its southern parts of Scorpio, then are moistening

If The middle parts of Sagittarius, then are temperate
If The hinder parts of Sagittarius, then are fiery

If The southern parts of Sagittarius, then variation and moisture

If its middle parts of Capricorn, then are temperate
If its latter parts of Capricorn, then promote rain
If Both its northern and southern parts of Capricorn, then are injurious by means of moisture

If The middle parts of Aquarius, then are temperate
If The latter parts of Aquarius, then productive of wind
If The northern parts of Aquarius, then are heating
If The southern parts of Aquarius, then cause snow

If The middle parts of Pisces, then are moistening
If The hinder parts of Pisces, then highly heating
If The northern parts of Pisces, then excite wind
If The southern parts of Pisces, then are

If new or full Moon which may happen before the period of the Sun’s transit through either tropical or equinoctial point, then arrange the angles (as in the case of a nativity) according to the degree and hour at which the new or full Moon may be found to happen, in every latitude for which the consideration may be desired

If Such planets and stars as may have dominion over the places where the said new or full Moon happens, and over the following angle, then are then to be noted, in the same manner as that stated with regard to eclipses
If The new or full Moon first taking place on the Sun’s progress through each sign, then observation of the new or full Moon

If a new Moon should have happened at a period nearest to the Sun’s transit over the past tropical or equinoctial point, then the new Moon alsoin each succeeding sign, until the commencement of the next quarter, are to be observed
If a full Moon should have so happened, then similar observation is to be made of each subsequent full Moon

If The angles, also, must be duly attended to, as well as the planets and stars ruling in both the places, then peculiar qualities of the two places, and the winds, liable to be excited by the planets themselves and by those parts of the signs in which they may be situated, are likewise to be considered

If the partile configurations of the Sun and Moon, at the intermediate quarters, as well as at the new or full Moon, then there is a certain variation in the constitution of the atmosphere, which usually commences about three days before, and sometimes, also, about three days after the Moon has equated her course to the Sun

If The configurations effected between the Moon, at each quarterly equation, and the planets, whether by the trine, sextile, or other authorized distances, then the peculiar property of the change in the constitutions of the atmosphere depends much upon such configurations, and may be accordingly perceived by considering the nature of the influence which the said configurated planets and the signs exercise over the atmosphere and the winds
If Planets in trine, sextile, or other authorized distances, then Peculiar property of the change in the constitutions of the atmosphere
If Brighter and more efficacious fixed stars near the Sun, either matutine or vespertine, then Convert the constitution of the atmosphere to an agreement with their own natures
If Luminaries transit any one of the angles, then A similar effect is also produced (convert the constitution of the atmosphere to an agreement with their own natures)
If Certain positions of the Moon, then The flux and reflux of the sea are caused
If Luminaries in angles, then A change of the wind is produced, according to the direction of the Moon’s ecliptical latitude
If Sun free from mists, gloom, and clouds, then Serene weather
If Sun have a wavering or fiery orb, or seem to emit or attract red rays, then Violent winds

If Sun accompanied in any one part by the clouds called parhelia, or by other reddish clouds of extended figure, in the form of long rays, then Violent winds, chiefly liable to arise from those parts in which the said phenomena may have shown themselves
If Sun should be pale or lurid, and rise or set encumbered with clouds, or surrounded by halos, then Storms and winds coming from the quarter of his apparent situation
If Sun be also accompanied by parhelia, or by lurid or dark rays, then Similar effects are also threatened from the parts where those appearances may be situated (Storms and winds)
If Moon shine thin and clear, with no other phenomena about her, at the third day before or after her conjunction with the Sun, her opposition, and her intermediate quarters, then Serenity
If Moon appear thin and red, and have her whole unilluminated part visible, and in a state of vibration, then Winds from the quarter of her latitude and declination
If Moon appear dark, or pale and thick, then Threatens storms and showers
If One halo around the Moon, bright and clear, and decaying by degrees, then Promises serene weather
If Two or three halos appear around the Moon, then Tempests are indicated
If Halos around the Moon seem reddish and broken, then Threaten tempests, with violent and boisterous winds
If Halos around the Moon dark and thick, then Foreshow storms and snow
If Halos around the Moon black and broken, then Tempests with both winds and snow
If A greater number of halos appear around the Moon, then Storms of greater fury are portended
If Planets, also, and the brighter fixed stars, occasionally have halos, then Indicate certain effects appropriate to their tinctures, and to the nature of the stars around which they may be situated
If Stars appear brighter and larger than usual, then Indicate an excitation of the wind from that quarter in which they may be situated
If The nebulous mass of the Presepe in Cancer, and others similar to it, appear gloomy and indistinct, or thick, in fine weather, then Threaten a fall of rain
If The nebulous mass of the Presepe in Cancer, and others similar to it, appear clear and in continual vibration, then Announce rough gales of wind
If Appearances occasionally visible in the sky, resembling the trains of comets, then Indicate wind and drought ; in a degree proportionate to their multitude and continuance
If Appearances, resembling shooting or falling stars, when presented in one part only, then Threaten a movement of wind from that part
If Appearances, resembling shooting or falling stars, when presented in various and opposite parts, then Portend the approach of all kinds of tempestuous weather, together with thunder and lightning
If Clouds resembling fleeces of wool, then Presage tempests
If The occasional appearance of the rainbow in stormy weather, then The approach of serenity
If The occasional appearance of the rainbow in fine weather, then Storms
If If the northern of the two stars, situated one on each side of the Presepe, and called the Asini, should not appear, then The north wind will blow
If If the southern one of the two stars, situated one on each side of the Presepe, and called the Asini, be invisible, then The south wind
If Configuration of the stars at the time of birth, then Acts in signification

If A planet is connected with a degree by triplicity, house, exaltation, terms, and phase or configuration, then The exact degree which it occupies in that sign in which it may be posited during the parturition, is to be remarked; and it is then to be inferred that a degree of the same numerical denomination was actually ascending, at the precise time of birth, in that sign which appears, by the Doctrine of Ascensions, to be nearest to the ascendant.
If The Sun and Saturn are allotted to the person of the father, then Indicates the situation of affairs affecting the parents
If The Moon and Venus are allotted to the person of the mother, then Indicates the situation of affairs affecting the parents

If Luminaries are accompanied by benefics in the same signs or the signs next following, and by stars of the same tendency, and well established in the prerogatives, then A conspicuous and brilliant fortune is presaged
If The Sun is attended by matutine stars, then A conspicuous and brilliant fortune is presaged
If The Moon is attended by vespertine stars, then A conspicuous and brilliant fortune is presaged
If Saturn is matutine and in proper face or in an angle, then Foreshows the prosperity of the father
If Venus is matutine and in proper face or in an angle, then Foreshows the prosperity of the mother
If The luminaries hold no connection with the planets and are unattended by any doryphory, then The adverse fortunes of the parents, their humble state and obscurity, are denoted
If Saturn and Venus may not be favourably constituted, then The adverse fortunes of the parents, their humble state and obscurity, are denoted
If The luminaries have a doryphory of a condition or tendency foreign to their own, then The parents are subjected to a state of vicissitude, never rising above mediocrity
If Mars ascends near in succession to the Sun, then The parents are subjected to a state of vicissitude, never rising above mediocrity
If Saturn ascends near in succession to the Moon, then The parents are subjected to a state of vicissitude, never rising above mediocrity

If The benefics are found constituted unfavourably, and not in conformity with their own natural condition and tendency, then The parents are subjected to a state of vicissitude, never rising above mediocrity
If The part of fortune is in a favourable position and in consonance with the doryphory of the Sun and Moon, then The estate of the parents will then remain steady and secure
If The position of the part of fortune is discordant and adverse, then The parents’ estate will be unproductive and even burthensome
If The malefics compose the doryphory, then The parents’ estate will be unproductive and even burthensome
If Jupiter or Venus is configurated with either the Sun or Saturn, then A long life is presaged for the father
If Saturn makes an harmonious configuration with the Sun (conjunction, sextile, or trine), then A long life is presaged for the father
If Mars is elevated above, or ascends in succession to the Sun or to Saturn, then The father is liable to infirmities
If Saturn is not in consonance with the Sun, but configurated with it by the quartile or opposition, then The father is liable to infirmities
If Both Saturn and the Sun are posited in cadent houses, then The father is liable to infirmities

If Mars is elevated above, or ascends in succession to the Sun or to Saturn, and Saturn is not in consonance with the Sun, but configurated with it by the quartile or opposition, and both Saturn and the Sun are posited in cadent houses, then The father is liable to infirmities
If Mars is elevated above, or ascends in succession to the Sun or to Saturn, then The father will live only a short life, and suffer from various bodily injuries and diseases
If Saturn is not in consonance with the Sun, but configurated with it by the quartile or opposition, then The father will live only a short life, and suffer from various bodily injuries and diseases
If Both Saturn and the Sun are posited in angles or succedent houses, then The father will live only a short life, and suffer from various bodily injuries and diseases

If Mars is elevated above, or ascends in succession to the Sun or to Saturn, and Saturn is not in consonance with the Sun, but configurated with it by the quartile or opposition, and both Saturn and the Sun are posited in angles or succedent houses, then The father will live only a short life, and suffer from various bodily injuries and diseases
If The Sun and Saturn are in the ascendant and the mid-heaven, or in their succedent houses, then The shortness of the father's life is particularly intimated
If The Sun and Saturn are in the western and the lower heaven, or in the houses succedent thereto, then The father's affliction by diseases and injuries is particularly intimated
If Mars is aspected to the Sun, then The father will die suddenly, or receive injury in his face or eyes

If Mars is aspected to Saturn, then The father will be afflicted with contractions of the muscles or limbs, and with fevers and disorders proceeding from inflammation and wounds; or even death may be the consequence
If Saturn is badly configurated with the Sun, then Saturn will also inflict disease and death on the father, by inducing such particular disorders as are incidental from watery humour
If The Sun is configurated with the Moon or Venus, then The mother will live long
If Venus is harmoniously configurated with the Moon (sextile, trine, or the conjunction), then The mother will live long
If Mars is succedent to the Moon and Venus, or in quartile or opposition to them, then Adverse accidents and disease will attend the mother
If Saturn is similarly aspected to the Moon only, and both of them be void of course or retrograde, or cadent, then Adverse accidents and disease will attend the mother

If Mars is succedent to the Moon and Venus, or in quartile or opposition to them, or, if Saturn be similarly aspected to the Moon only, and both of them be void of course or retrograde, or cadent, then Adverse accidents and disease will attend the mother
If The Moon and Venus are swift in motion and placed in angles, then Her life will be short, or grievously afflicted
If The Moon and Venus are in the oriental angles, or succedent houses, then Particularly denotes the shortness of her life
If The Moon and Venus are in those which are occidental, then Her affliction
If Mars is aspected to the Moon and the Moon is oriental, then The mother’s sudden death, or some injury in her face or eyes, will be produced
If Mars is aspected to the Moon and the Moon is occidental, then Death will be occasioned by miscarriage in parturition, by inflammation, or by wounds
If Mars is aspected to Venus, then Death will then take place from fever, some latent disease, or sudden sickness
If Saturn's aspect to the Moon, when she is oriental, then Inflicts on the mother disease and death from extreme colds, or fevers
If Saturn's aspect to the Moon, when she is occidental, then The danger arises from affections of the womb, or from consumption
If Moon is occidental, then danger arises from affections of the womb, or from consumption

If nativity is diurnal, then for the father, observe the degree the Sun is in, in the child’s nativity; and make that the degree ascending for the father; and conformable to that, order the cusps of all the other houses
If nativity is diurnal, then for the mother, use Venus
If nativity is nocturnal, then for the father, take the place of Saturn
If nativity is nocturnal, then for the mother, that of the Moon
If place is configurated with the benefics, then there will be several brothers and sisters
If place is configurated with the benefics and in bicorporeal signs, then birth of several brothers and sisters
If place is configurated with the benefics and in signs of single form, then birth of several brothers and sisters
If malefics should be in elevation over this place, then the brothers and sisters will then be few in number
If malefics are hostilely situated in opposition thereto, then the brothers and sisters will then be few in number
If malefics may surround the Sun, then this fewness especially follows
If hostile configuration be presented from the other angles, then the brothers and sisters will then be few in number
If hostile configuration be presented from the ascendant, then the brothers and sisters will then be few in number
If Saturn from the ascendant, then Saturn will then represent the elder born
If Mars from the ascendant, then Mars, by inflicting death, will diminish the total number of brothers and sisters
If stars, which promise brethren, be favourably circumstanced as to their cosmical position, then the brethren will be eminent and illustrious
If stars, which promise brethren, be of an adverse nature, then the brethren will be humble and obscure
If malefic stars should be in elevation over those which give brethren, then the life of the brethren will then be only of short duration
If stars, constituted masculinely, then represent brothers
If stars, constituted femininely, then represent sisters
If more oriental stars, then represent the elder born
If those which are more occidental, then the younger
If stars, which give brethren, be harmoniously configurated with that one which has dominion of the sign allotted to brethren, then the brethren will be mutually friendly and affectionate
If harmonious configuration be also extended, by the same planets, to the part of fortune, then the brethren will live together in communion
If stars, which give brethren, should, on the contrary, be in situations unconnected with each other, then the brethren will then live at variance, mutually practising enmity and fraud
If stars, which give brethren, should, on the contrary, be in opposition, then the brethren will then live at variance, mutually practising enmity and fraud
If planets in the east, then masculinely disposed
If planets in the west, then femininely disposed
If planets should be matutine, then they are considered to signify the male gender
If planets should be vespertine, then the female
If two, or all three, of the said places may be situated in bicorporeal signs, then births of this kind will occur, in consequence of the combination which then arises
If all the planets, which control those places, should also be similarly circumstanced, then births of this kind will occur, in consequence of the combination which then arises
If only some of them be posited in bicorporeal signs, while the rest may be placed by two or more together, then births of this kind will occur, in consequence of the combination which then arises
If all the ruling places may be in bicorporeal signs, most of the planets being, at the same time, posited in the same way, and configurated with them, then even more than twins will be born
If Saturn, Jupiter, and Mars may be configurated with the places before appointed, in bicorporeal signs, then three males will be born
If Venus and the Moon, with Mercury femininely constituted, may be configurated in likemanner, then three females will be born
If Saturn, Jupiter, and Venus may be configurated, then two males and one female will be born
If Venus, the Moon, and Mars may be so configurated, then two females and one male
If Luminaries are cadent from the ascendant or not configurated with it, and angles are occupied by malefics, then Monstrous or defective birth
If Rulers of the luminaries, the Moon, and Mercury are in places totally inconjunct and unconnected with the places of the preceding new or full Moon and its ruler, then Monstrous birth

If Luminaries are in quadrupedal or bestial signs, and the two malefics in angles, and there is an absence of connection between the rulers of the luminaries, the Moon, and Mercury with the places of the preceding new or full Moon and its ruler, then Non-human birth
If Luminaries are not supported by any benefic planet, but only by malefics, then Creature born will be wholly indocile, wild, and of evil nature
If Luminaries receive support from Jupiter or Venus, then Offspring will be like that of dogs or cats, or other creatures held in religious veneration and used in worship
If Mercury supports the luminaries, then Offspring will resemble that of fowls, oxen, or swine, or, of other animals adapted to the service of mankind

If Luminaries are in signs of human shape, while other circumstances in the scheme of the nativity may exist as before described, then Creature born will then be human, or will partake of human nature, although it will still be defective in some peculiar quality
If No benefic planet should lend support to any one of the prescribed places, then Offspring produced will be utterly void of reason, and indeed indefinable

If Jupiter or Venus give support, then Defect will be veiled by a specious outward appearance, similar to that of hermaphrodites, and of those persons called Harpocratiaci, or others of like imperfections
If Mercury also give support, in addition to that of Jupiter or Venus, then Offspring will then become an interpreter of oracles and divinations
If Mercury support alone, then Offspring will be deaf and dumb, although clever and ingenious in its intellect

If Either of the two luminaries is in an angle, and one of the malefics is either in conjunction with that luminary, or else distant in longitude from each luminary, in an exactly equal space; so as to form the point of junction of two equal sides of a triangle, of which sides the two luminaries form the extremities, while, at the same time, no benefic star may partake in the configuration, and while the rulers of the luminaries may be also posited in places belonging to, or controlled by, the malefics, then Child will not be susceptible of nurture, but will immediately perish

If The rays of two malefics may nearly approach the places of the two luminaries, casting an injurious influence either on both, or only one of them, and if both the said malefics be together succedent, or in opposition, to the luminaries, or if one of them be succedent, and the other in opposition, or even if only one may particularly afflict one of the luminaries, then No duration of life will be allotted to the child
If Mars is succedent to the Sun, then Exceedingly pernicious
If Saturn is succedent to the Moon, then Exceedingly pernicious
If Saturn is in opposition to the Sun, or in elevation above them, then The Sun will then be afflicted by Saturn
If Mars is in opposition to the Moon, or in elevation above them, then The Moon will then be afflicted by Mars
If Saturn or Mars should have local prerogatives in the signs containing the luminaries, or in the sign on the ascendant, then The Sun will then be afflicted by Saturn, and the Moon by Mars
If Luminaries are placed in two opposite angles, and by the two malefics being each so posited as to be equally distant from each luminary, then Child will be born almost, if not quite, dead

If Luminaries should be separating from, or be otherwise configurated with benefic planets, whose rays may be projected to parts preceding the said luminaries, then Child will then live as many days, or hours, as there are degrees, numbered between the prorogator and the nearest malefic

If Malefics should cast their rays to parts preceding the luminaries, and benefics to parts following them, then Child will be abandoned at its birth; but will afterwards meet with adoption, and will live
If Malefics should be in elevation above those benefics which are thus configurated, then Child, so adopted, will lead a life of misery and servitude
If Benefics should be in elevation, then Whoever may adopt the deserted child will supply the place of its parents
If A benefic planet should either ascend with, or near in succession to the Moon, or be applying to her, then Implied positive outcome regarding the child's life
If benefics should be in elevation, then whoever may adopt the deserted child will supply the place of its parents
If a benefic planet should either ascend with, or near in succession to the Moon, or be applying to her, then the child’s own parents will, in that case, take it again under their protection

If one of those planets, which may be configurated towards the production of two, or even more children, should be under the west, then the children will be born half dead, or deformed, and imperfect in body
If the planet so situated should also be beneath the malefics, then the children will not be susceptible of nurture, or their life will be of the shortest span
If places of the malefics, Saturn and Mars, meeting the prorogator bodily, or by emission of rays in quartile, from either side, or in opposition in succeeding signs, then aneretic
If places of the malefics, Saturn and Mars, meeting the prorogator by a sextile ray in a sign of equal power, obeying or beholding the sign of the prorogator in succeeding signs, then aneretic
If degree, in signs following, in quartile with the prorogatory place, then aneretic
If degree in sextile, if badly afflicted, then aneretic
If degree in trine, if in signs of short ascension, then aneretic
If Sun's place, should the Moon be prorogatory, then aneretic
If places brought to the prorogatory place are badly afflicted, then their anzretic tendency is effectual
If places are situated within the terms of a benefic, then the operation of their anzretic degree becomes impeded

If either of the benefics should cast a ray in quartile, trine, or opposition to the said anzretic degree itself, or to some other degree near in succession, and not farther distant from it than twelve degrees, if the benefic be Jupiter, then the operation of their anzretic degree becomes impeded

If either of the benefics should cast a ray in quartile, trine, or opposition to the said anzretic degree itself, or to some other degree near in succession, and not farther distant from it than eight, if Venus, then the operation of their anzretic degree becomes impeded
If both the prorogator and its opponent should be bodies, and not have the same latitude, then the operation of their anzretic degree becomes impeded

If Moon is not prorogatory, the solar place itself is shackled by the simultaneous presence of a malefic, and not restored to freedom of operation by any benefic, then solar place itself becomes anzretic

If a prorogatory and preceding place is on the ascendant and the succeeding place devolves into the preceding place, then the succeeding place will devolve into the preceding place after the times of ascension of Aries and Taurus
If the first point of Aries is placed on the midheaven, culminating, then the whole distance is to be reckoned, in which Aries and Taurus pass the mid-heaven
If the first point of Aries is descending, then the first point of Gemini will arrive at its future descension from the meridian
If Places of both significators are afflicted, and the transit of the stars, at the then existing ingress, operate injuriously on the chief ruling places, then Death will ensue
If One of the places may be disposed favourably to human nature, then The crisis will still be important and perilous
If Both the places be so disposed favourably, then Some debility only, or transient malady, or hurt, will then happen
If Constellations of human form, both those within and those without the zodiac, then Act in favour of giving a handsome shape to the body, and due proportion to the figure

If Constellations not of human form, then Vary its due proportions, and incline it towards their own shape; assimilating it, in some measure, to their own peculiarities, either by enlarging or diminishing its size, by giving it additional strength or weakness, or by otherwise improving or disfiguring it
If Leo, Virgo, and Sagittarius, then Enlarge the person
If Pisces, Cancer, and Capricorn, then Tend to make it diminutive
If The upper and anterior parts of Aries, Taurus, and Leo, then Increase its strength
If Their lower and posterior parts of Aries, Taurus, and Leo, then Render it weaker
If The anterior parts of Sagittarius, Scorpio, and Gemini, then Produce greater debility
If The posterior parts of Sagittarius, Scorpio, and Gemini, then Produce greater vigour
If Virgo, Libra, and Sagittarius, then Contribute to render the person handsome and well-proportioned
If Scorpio, Pisces, and Taurus, then Incline it to be misshapen and disfigured
If Virgo, Libra, and Sagittarius, then render the person handsome and well-proportioned
If Scorpio, Pisces, and Taurus, then incline it to be misshapen and disfigured

If malefic planets stationed bodily on any of the successive degrees composing the ascendant or the descendant, or configurated with such degrees in quartile or in opposition, then some bodily disorders or injuries will attach to the native or person then born

If both luminaries, either together or in opposition, or even if one of them, should be angularly posited in the manner described, then some bodily disorders or injuries will attach to the native or person then born
If a malefic which may have ascended in succession to the luminaries, but also any one which may have preascended, if placed in an angle, then has power to inflict certain diseases and injuries
If Saturn, then rules the right ear, the spleen, the bladder, the phlegm, and the bones
If Jupiter, then governs the hand, the lungs, the arteries, and the seed
If Mars, then rules the left ear, the kidneys, the veins, and the privities
If Sun, then rules the eyes, the brain, the heart, the sinews or nerves, and all the right side
If Venus, then rules the nostrils, the liver, and the flesh
If Mercury, then rules the speech, the understanding, the bile, the tongue, and the fundament
If Moon, then governs the palate, the throat, the stomach, the belly, the womb, and all the left parts
If malefics may be oriental, then some casual hurt, or injurious affection of the body, is the utmost that takes effect
If malefics may be occidental, then considerable diseases occur
If Moon may be in the angles, either operating her conjunction, or being at the full and configurated with any one of the nebulous collections in the zodiac, then blindness of one eye will ensue
If Moon be in an angle, and in her decrease, and Mars or Saturn, being matutine, ascend in succession to her, then both eyes will be injured

If Sun be in an angle, and Mars or Saturn pre-ascend before him, and be configurated with both luminaries, whether the luminaries be in one and the same sign, or in opposition, then both eyes will be injured
If Mars will cause blindness, then by a stroke or blow, or by the sword or by burning
If Mars be configurated with Mercury, then blindness will be effected either in a place of exercise or sport, or by the assault of robbers
If Saturn, then produces blindness by cataract, or cold, by a white film, or by other similar disorders

If Venus in the west, and Saturn be in conjunction or in configuration with her, or be changing place with her, while Mars, at the same time, is in elevation above her, or in opposition to her, then will produce impotence in the native, if a male; and, if a female, will render her liable to abortion, or to produce children stillborn, or not capable of being extracted except in mangled parts

If Venus be also configurated with Mercury, as well as Saturn, Mars again being in elevation above her, or in opposition to her, then the native will be either an eunuch or hermaphrodite, or devoid of the natural channels and vents

If Sun also partake in the configuration, the luminaries and Venus being all masculinely constituted, the Moon in her decrease, and the malefics brought up in the degrees next successively ascending, then the males will be born maimed or crippled, or injured in their private members (particularly under Aries, Leo, Scorpio, Capricorn, and Aquarius); and the females will remain childless and unprolific. And ‘it also occasionally happens that the natives, under such a configuration, are likewise injured in the face or eyes
If Saturn and Mercury, in conjunction with the Sun, be in the angles, then the native will have some defect in the tongue, and stammer or speak with difficulty
If Mercury be occidental, and both he and Saturn configurated with the Moon, then the native will have some defect in the tongue, and stammer or speak with difficulty
If Mars be found together with Saturn and Mercury, then he will for the most part remove the defect in the tongue, after the Moon shall have completed her approach to him
If malefics be in angles, and the luminaries, either together or in opposition, be brought up to them, then the body will then be afflicted with excrescences, distortions, lameness or paralysis

If malefics be brought up to the luminaries, especially when the Moon may be in her nodes, or in her bend, or in obnoxious signs, such as Aries, Taurus, Cancer, Scorpio, and Capricorn, then the body will then be afflicted with excrescences, distortions, lameness or paralysis
If malefics be in conjunction with the luminaries, then the calamity will take effect from the very moment of birth

If malefics be in the mid-heaven, in elevation above the luminaries, or in opposition to each other, then it will then arise out of some great and dangerous accident ; such as a fall from some height or precipice, an attack of robbers, or of quadrupeds
If Mars hold dominion, then he will produce the misfortune by means of fire or wounds, through quarrels, or by robbers
If Saturn, then it will be caused by a fall, by shipwreck, or by convulsive fits or spasms
If Moon’s being posited in a tropical or equinoctial sign, then the minor bodily disorders mostly occur
If Moon in the vernal equinox, then these disorders usually arise from the white leprosy
If Moon in the summer tropic, then these disorders usually arise from tetters
If Moon in the autumnal equinox, then these disorders usually arise from leprosy
If Moon in the winter tropic, then these disorders usually arise from the eruption of pimples, and similar inconveniences

If malefics may be configurated in the same situations as those before prescribed, yet differing in one respect ; that is to say, being occidental of the Sun and oriental of the Moon, then considerable diseases take effect

If Saturn, then will generally produce cold in the bowels, excessive phlegm, rheumatism, emaciation, sickliness, jaundice, dysentry, cough, obstruction, colic, or scurvy; and, in women, besides these diseases, he produces complaints of the womb

If Mars, then will cause expectoration of blood, atrabilarious attacks, pulmonary complaints, sores, and diseases in the private parts (which will be rendered still more painful by surgical burning or incision), such as fistula, hemorrhoids, or knots in the fundament, and also inflamed and putrifying ulcers. In women, to these calamities, he adds abortion, excision of the fcetus or its mortification

If Mercury be in familiarity with Saturn, then he will much augment the coldness, and promote the continuance of rheumatism, and the disturbance of the fluids; especially in the chest, throat, and stomach
If Mercury be in familiarity with Mars, then he will tend to produce greater dryness, and will increase ulcers
If Mercury in familiarity with Saturn, then augment the coldness, and promote the continuance of rheumatism, and the disturbance of the fluids; especially in the chest, throat, and stomach

If Mercury in familiarity with Mars, then tend to produce greater dryness, and will increase ulcers, abscesses, loss of hair, scarified sores, erysipelas, tetters, blackness of bile, insanity, epilepsy, and similar disorders
If Cancer, Capricorn, and Pisces, then appropriately cause diseases of putridity, tetters, excoriation, scrofula, fistula, leprosy, and the like
If Sagittarius and Gemini, then produce disease by falling fits and epilepsy
If planets posited in the latter degrees of the signs, then the extremities of the body will then be chiefly affected by the disease or hurt
If disease or hurt with no benefics in configuration with the malefics which effect the evil, nor with the luminaries posited in angles, then incurable
If disease or hurt with benefics configurated, but malefics well fortified and in elevation above them, then incapable of remedy
If benefics hold principal situations, and be in elevation above the obnoxious malefics, then the disease or hurt will then be moderate, and have neither deformity nor disgrace attached to it
If benefics be oriental, then the disease or hurt will sometimes be altogether prevented and set aside
If Jupiter, then will conceal and soothe hurts and diseases by means of human aid, such as wealth or rank can command
If Jupiter joined with Mercury, then the assistance will be further improved by the addition of skilful physicians and good medicine
If Venus, then will cause hurts to appear in a manner neither ungraceful nor unbecoming, and will ameliorate diseases by medicines granted by the
If Saturn present in the configuration, then the afflicted persons will move abroad to show their maladies, and to complain
If Saturn and Mercury present in the configuration, then the afflicted persons will move abroad to show their maladies, and to complain for the sake of deriving support and profit from the exhibition

If tropical signs, then dispose the mind to enter much into political matters, rendering it eager to engage in public and turbulent affairs, fond of distinction, and busy in theology ; at the same time, ingenious, acute, inquisitive, inventive, speculative, and studious of astronomy and divination

If Bicorporeal signs, then render the mind variable, versatile, not easy to be understood, volatile, and unsteady; inclined to duplicity, amorous, wily, fond of music, careless, full of expedients, and regretful

If Fixed signs, then make the mind just, uncompromising, constant, firm of purpose, prudent, patient, industrious, strict, chaste, mindful of injuries, steady in pursuing its object, contentious, desirous of honour, seditious, avaricious, and pertinacious
If Oriental positions, and those in the ascendant, especially if made by planets in their proper faces, then make men liberal, frank, self-confident, brave, ingenious, unreserved, yet acute

If Oriental stations, and positions on the mid-heaven, or culminations, then make men reflective, constant, of good memory, firm, prudent, magnanimous, successful in pursuing their desires, inflexible, powerful in intellect, strict, not easily imposed upon, judicious, active, hostile to crime, and skilful in science

If Precedent and occidental positions, then make men unsteady, irreverent, imbecile, impatient of labour, easily impressed, humble, doubting, wavering, boastful, and cowardly, slothful, lazy, and hard to rouse

If Occidental stations, and positions on the lower heaven (as well as Mercury and Venus, when making vespertine descension by day, and rising in the night), then will render the mind ingenious and sagacious, but not capable of great recollection, nor very industrious; yet inquisitive in occult matters, such as magic and sacred mysteries; also studious of mechanics, and mechanical instruments: addicted to the observation of meteors, to philosophy, to augury by means of birds, and to the judgment of dreams
If planets having dominion be in places of their own, and in conditions suitable to their own qualities, then the mental properties will be rendered exquisite, unimpeded, and successful

If planets rule at the same time over both places ; that is to say, be by some mode configurated with Mercury, and holds separation from, or application to the Moon, then the mental properties will be rendered exquisite, unimpeded, and successful

If planets posited in places not particularly appropriate to themselves, then they will yet, even then, infuse into the composition of the mental energy the properties of their own nature; but obscurely and imperfectly, and not with such force and strong evidence as in the other case

If persons, who, in consequence of the familiarity of the malefics, become wicked and dishonest, have their impulse to commit evil, free and unrestrained, when the said familiarity is not governed by any contrary influence, then impulse to commit evil, free and unrestrained
If a contrary condition impede and govern that familiarity, then the impulse will be frustrated, and the culprits will be easily overtaken, and undergo punishment

If persons endowed with goodness and virtue, by the familiarity between the benefics and the before-mentioned places, and when no contrary influence in elevation may interpose, then will exert themselves with cheerfulness and alacrity in performing good actions, will be subject to no injustice, but enjoy the advantages of their honesty and virtue

If this familiarity should be superseded by some contrary condition, then the very mildness and humanity of these persons will operate to their disadvantage, exposing them to contempt and accusation, and rendering them liable to be wronged by the multitude

If Saturn, when alone possessing dominion of the mind, and governing Mercury and the Moon, and if posited in glory, both cosmically and with respect to the angles, then will make men careful of their bodies, strong and profound in opinion, austere, singular in their modes of thinking, laborious, imperious, hostile to crime, avaricious, parsimonious, accumulators of wealth, violent, and envious

If Saturn, when alone possessing dominion of the mind, and governing Mercury and the Moon, and if he be not in glory, cosmically, and as regards the angles, then he will debase the mind, making it penurious, pusillanimous, ill-disposed, indiscriminating, malignant, timorous, slanderous, fond of solitude, repining, incapable of shame, bigoted, fond of labour, void of natural affection, treacherous in friendship and in family connections, incapable of enjoyment, and regardless of the body

If Saturn connected with Jupiter in the mode before-mentioned, being also situated in glory, then Saturn will render the mind virtuous, respectful, well-intentioned, ready to assist, judicious, frugal, magnanimous, obliging, solicitous of good, affectionate in all d

If Saturn well Placed in elevation and in possession of dignities, then the mind virtuous, respectful, well-intentioned, ready to assist, judicious, frugal, magnanimous, obliging, solicitous of good, affectionate in all domestic ties, mild, prudent, patient, and philosophical

If Saturn connected and posited ingloriously, then men outrageous, incapable of learning, timorous, highly superstitious, yet regardless of religion, suspicious, averse to children, incapable of friendship, cunning, misjudging, faithless, foolishly wicked, irascible, hypocritical, idle and useless, without ambition, yet regretful, morose, highly reserved, over-cautious, and dull

If Saturn Conciliated with Mars, and posited in glory, then men reckless, over-diligent, free in speech, turbulent, boastful, austere in their dealings, pitiless, contemptuous, fierce, warlike, bold, fond of tumults, insidious, deceitful, and implacable; promoters of faction, tyrannical, rapacious, hostile to the commonwealth, delighting in strife, vindictive, profound in guilt, strenuous, impatient, insolent, mischievous, overbearing, evil, unjust, obstinate, inhuman, inflexible, immutable in opinion, busy, able in office, active, submitting to no opposition, and on the whole successful in their undertakings

If Saturn connected with Mars, and not placed in glory, then men plunderers, robbers, adulterers, submissive to evil, seeking gain by their turpitude, infidels in religion, void of the common affections, mischievous, treacherous, thievish, perjurers, and sanguinary ; eaters of unlawful food, familiar with guilt, assassins, sorcerers, sacrilegious, impious, violators of the tomb, and, in short, thoroughly depraved

If Saturn Conciliated with Venus, and being again in glory, then men averse to women, and renders them fond of governing, prone to solitude, highly reserved, regardless of rank, indifferent to beauty, envious, austere, unsociable, singular in opinion, addicted to divination and to religious services and mysteries ; solicitous of the priesthood, fanatical, and subservient to religion ; solemn, reverential, sedate, studious of wisdom, faithful in friendship, continent, reflective, circumspect, and scrupulous in regard to female virtue
