If Saturn conciliated with Venus, and not posited in glory, then men licentious and libidinous, practisers of lewdness, careless, and impure in sexual intercourse; obscene, treacherous to women, especially to those of their own families ; wanton, quarrelsome, sordid, hating elegance; slanderous, drunken, superstitious, adulterous, and impious ; blasphemers of the gods, and scoffers at holy rites ; calumniators, sorcerers, hesitating at nothing

If Saturn conciliated with Mercury, and if in a glorious position, then men inquisitive, loquacious, studious of law and of medicine, mystical, confederate in secrecy, fabricators of miracles, impostors, improvident, cunning, familiar with business, quick in perception, petulant, accurate, vigilant, meditative, fond of employment, and tractable

If Saturn connected with Mercury, and not posited gloriously, then men frivolous, vindictive, laborious, alienated from their families, fond of tormenting, and void of enjoyment; night-wanderers, insidious, treacherous, pitiless, and thievish ; magicians, sorcerers, forgers of writings, cheats, unsuccessful in their undertakings, and quickly reduced to adversity

If Jupiter alone has dominion of the mind, and is gloriously situated, then it renders it generous, gracious, pious, reverent, joyous, courteous, lofty, liberal, just, magnanimous, noble, self-acting, compassionate, fond of learning, beneficent, benevolent, and calculated for government

If Jupiter posited ingloriously, then he will endow the mind with qualities apparently similar to these, but not of such virtue and lustre : as, instead, of generosity, he will then cause profusion; instead of piety, bigotry ; for modesty, timidity ; for nobleness, arrogance ; for courteousness, folly ; for elegance, voluptuousness ; for magnanimity, carelessness ; and for liberality, indifference

If Jupiter Conciliated with Mars, and being in glory, then Jupiter will make men rough, warlike, skilful in military affairs, dictatorial, refractory, impetuous, daring, free in speech, able in action, fond of disputation, contentious, imperious, generous, ambitious, irascible, judicious, and fortunate

If Jupiter connected with Mars, and not placed in glory, then men mischievous, reckless, cruel, pitiless, seditious, quarrelsome, perverse, calumnious, arrogant, avaricious, tapacious, inconstant, vain and empty, unsteady, precipitate, faithless, injudicious, inconsiderate, senseless, and officious ; inculpators, prodigals, triflers, altogether without conduct, and giving way to every impulse

If Jupiter conciliated with Venus, and in a glorious position, then Jupiter will render the mind pure, joyous, delighting in elegance, in the arts and sciences, and in poetry and music; valuable in friendship, sincere, beneficent, compassionate, inoffensive, religious, fond of sports and exercises, prudent, amiable, and affectionate, gracious, noble, brilliant, candid, liberal, discreet, temperate, modest, pious, just, fond of glory, and in all respects honourable and worthy

If Jupiter posited ingloriously, when so connected with Venus, then he makes men luxurious, soft, effeminate, fond of dancing, indulgent in expenses, incapable of managing women, yet amorous and lascivious; mean, slanderous, adulterous, fond of dress, dissolute, dull, wasteful, without energy, enervated, fond of personal adornment, womanish in mind, yet observant of holy rites and ceremonies, faithful, harmless, pleasant, affable, cheerful, and liberal to misfortune

If Jupiter connected with Mercury, and posited in glory, then Jupiter will render men fit for much business, fond of learning, and of geometry and the mathematics ; poetical, public orators, acute, temperate, well-disposed, skilful in counsel, politic, beneficent, able in government, pious, religious, valuable in all useful professions, benevolent, affectionate in their families, ready in acquiring knowledge, philosophical, and dignified

If Jupiter connected with Mercury, and placed ingloriously, then he will produce contrary effects, rendering men frivolous, empty, contemptible, credulous of falsehood, senseless, fanatical, trifling, petulant, affectors of wisdom, stupid, arrogant, pretenders in art, magicians, and vacillating: Yet he will also produce men skilled in various learning, and of strong memory, capable of imparting instruction, and pure in their enjoyments

If Mars alone having dominion of the mind, and placed with glory, then makes men noble, imperious, irascible, warlike, versatile, powerful in intellect, daring, bold, refractory, careless, obstinate, acute, selfconfident, contemptuous, tyrannical, strenuous, stern and able in government

If Mars posited ingloriously, then he makes men cruel, mischievous, sanguinary, tumultuous, extravagant in expense, boisterous, ruffian-like, precipitate, drunken, rapacious, pitiless, familiar with crime, restless, outrageous, hostile to their families, and infidels in religion

If Mars conciliated with Venus, and posited in glory, then he renders the mind cheerful, docile, friendly, complacent, joyous, playful, frank, delighting in songs and dancing, amorous, fond of the arts, and of dramatic personation, voluptuous, brave, libidinous in desire, sensible, cautious, and discreet ; disposed to free sexual intercourse,? quick in anger, extravagant in expense, and jealous

If Mars have an inglorious position when thus conciliated with Venus, then he makes men overbearing, lascivious, sordid, opprobious, adulterous, mischievous, liars, fabricators of deceit, cheats of their own families as well as others, eager in desire, and at the same time soon satiated, debauchers of wives and virgins, daring, impetuous, ungovernable, treacherous, faithless, ‘dangerous, fickle and weak in mind; and occasionally also wasteful, fond of dress, audacious, and shameless

If Mars connected with Mercury, and placed in glory, then Mars renders men skilful in command, cautious, strenuous, active, obstinate, yet versatile, inventive, sophistical, laborious, busy in all things, eloquent, imposing, deceitful, inconstant, overknowing, maliciously artful, quick witted, seductive, hypocritical, treacherous, habituated to evil, inquisitive, fond of strife, and successful; fair dealers with persons of habits similar to their own, and, in short, altogether mischievous to their enemies, though beneficial to their friends

If Mars be posited ingloriously, and thus connected with Mercury, then he makes men prodigal, yet avaricious, cruel, daring, bold, regretful and vacillating ; liars, thieves, infidels in religion, perjurers, and impostors ; seditious, incendiaries, frequenters of theatres, covered with infamy, robbers, housebreakers, sanguinary, forgers of writings, familiar with crime, jugglers, magicians, sorcerers, and assassins

If Venus rules alone in a position of glory, then she renders the mind benignant, good, voluptuous, copious in wit, pure, gay, fond of dancing, jealous, abhorring wickedness, delighting in the arts, pious, modest, well-disposed, happy in dreams, affectionate, beneficent, compassionate, refined in taste, easily reconciled, tractable, and entirely amiable
If Venus contrarily posited, then she renders the mind dull, amorous, effeminate, timorous, indiscriminating, sordid, faulty, obscure, and ignominious
If Venus conciliated with Mercury, and posited with glory, then makes men ~ TIpos μιξιν θηλειων και appevwv διακειμενους

If Venus is well-posited, then the native is in the arts, pious, modest, well-disposed, happy in dreams, affectionate, beneficent, compassionate, refined in taste, easily reconciled, tractable, and entirely amiable
If Venus is contrarily posited, then the native is dull, amorous, effeminate, timorous, indiscriminating, sordid, faulty, obscure, and ignominious
If Venus is conciliated with Mercury and posited with glory, then men are inclined to unnatural sexual relations

If The Moon and Mercury are unconnected with each other or with the oriental horizon, and planets adversely and noxiously configurated are in elevation above them, or overrule them, or are in opposition to them, then the mental properties will then consequently become impregnated with various disorders

If Mercury and the Moon are unconnected either with each other, or with the oriental horizon, while Saturn and Mars may be in angles and superintend the scheme; Saturn is so posited by day, then epilepsy generally attaches to all persons born

If Mercury and the Moon are unconnected either with each other, or with the oriental horizon, while Saturn and Mars may be in angles and superintend the scheme; Mars is so posited by night, then epilepsy generally attaches to all persons born
If Saturn has dominion by night, but Mars by day, especially if in Cancer, Virgo or Pisces, then the persons born will become insane

If the Moon, being in face to the Sun, should be governed by Saturn when operating her conjunction, but by Mars when effecting her opposition; and particularly when it may happen in Sagittarius and in Pisces, then they will become demoniac, and afflicted with moisture of the brain

If the malefics, only, should have ruled the scheme, in the manner described, then the said disorders of the mind will become irremediable, although at the same time not eminent, but doubtful, and not openly displayed

If the benefics, Jupiter and Venus, be conciliated, and be posited in eastern parts and in angles, while the malefics may be in western parts, then the disorders, although highly conspicuous, will then be susceptible to cure
If Jupiter is in influence, then they will be healed by means of medical or surgical aid, and by diet and medicine
If Venus is in influence, then they will be healed by the guidance of oracles and by divine intercession

If the benefics are occidental, and the malefics are found in eastern parts and in angles, then the disorders will then become not only incurable, but most conspicuous: the epileptic persons will then be subjected to constant fits, and to danger of death ; the insane will become outrageous and unmanageable, breaking away from their families, raving and wandering in nakedness: the demoniacs and those afflicted with moisture of the brain will become furious, uttering mysterious sayings, and wounding themselves
If the Sun and Mars contribute, then insanity
If Jupiter and Mercury contribute, then epilepsy
If Venus contributes, then the fury of enthusiasm
If Saturn and the Moon contribute, then demoniac affections and moisture of the brain

If When the Sun, instead of Mercury, may be with the Moon, and if Mars, together with Venus, be then in familiarity with them, in that case, provided the luminaries only be found in masculine signs, then men will excel in their nature, or, in other words, will possess in full plenitude the properties becoming their sex ; while the properties of women, who are thus constituted more masculinely and more actively, will deviate from the usual limits of nature

If both Mars and Venus, or if only one of them, be likewise masculinely situated, then men will be freely and promptly inclined to natural intercourse and connexion; and women will be, in like manner, licentious and intemperate in intercourse beyond nature
If only Venus is situated masculinely, then their desires will be practised in privacy, and not openly
If Mars also masculinely placed, together with, then shamelessly and publicly

If the luminaries only be in feminine signs, then women will then possess their natural functions in greater plenitude, and men will deviate from the limits of nature towards effeminacy and wantonness
If Venus be femininely posited, then women will be lustful and licentious, and men wanton and soft; seeking connexion contrary to nature; yet in privacy and not openly
If Mars be posited femininely, then they will then put their desires in practice shamelessly and
If The oriental and diurnal positions of Mars and Venus, then contribute to more masculine and more reputable qualities
If their occidental and vespertine positions, then contribute to qualities more feminine, and more sordid
If Saturn be in familiarity with them, then he will likewise co-operate, by tending to produce greater impurity and obscenity, and greater evil altogether
If Jupiter is in familiarity, then tends to greater decency and modesty, and altogether to better conduct
If Mercury is in familiarity, then tends to greater mobility, diversity, activity, and notoriety of the passions
If the planets which assume dominion of the Part of Fortune be in full force, and especially should the luminaries also give them suitable testimony in addition, then they will create much wealth
If Saturn is in influence over the fortune of wealth, then the acquirement of wealth by means of buildings, agriculture, or navigation
If Jupiter is in influence over the fortune of wealth, then by holding some government, or office of trust, or by the priesthood
If Mars is in influence over the fortune of wealth, then by the army and military command
If Venus is in influence over the fortune of wealth, then by means of friends, by the dowry of wives, or by other gifts proceeding from women
If Mercury is in influence over the fortune of wealth, then by the sciences and by trade

If Saturn, when thus in influence over the fortune of wealth, be also configurated with Jupiter, especially, if the configuration should exist in the superior angles, Jupiter being also in a bicorporeal sign and receiving the application of the Moon, then he particularly provides wealth through inheritance; especially, if the configuration should exist in the superior angles, Jupiter being also in a bicorporeal sign and receiving the application of the Moon ; for, in such a case, the native will also be adopted by persons unallied to him, and will become heir to their property
If other stars, of the same condition as those which rule the Part of Fortune, should likewise exhibit testimonies of dominion, then the wealth will be permanent
If stars of an adverse condition should either be in elevation above the ruling places, or ascend in succession to them, then the wealth will not continue

If should the two luminaries be found in masculine signs and in angles, or even if only one of them be in an angle, they being at the same time specially attended by a doryphory composed of all, then degree of rank or dignity
If The two luminaries are found in masculine signs and in angles., then Persons will become kings or princes.
If One of the luminaries is in an angle and the luminaries are in masculine signs., then Persons will become kings or princes.
If Attendant stars are also in angles., then Persons will become great, powerful, and mighty in the world.
If Attendant stars are configurated with the angle above the earth., then Persons will become great, powerful, and mighty in the world.
If Configurations made by the attendant stars with the angles above the earth are dexter., then Persons will become even yet more abundantly great, powerful, and mighty in the world.

If The Sun is in a masculine sign and the Moon is in a feminine sign, and only one of them is posited in an angle., then Persons will then become merely chieftains, invested with the sovereignty of life.

If The attendant stars are neither actually in angles, nor bear any testimony to the angles, while the luminaries are situated as above., then Persons will still enjoy eminence, will attain only some limited dignity or distinction.

If Neither of the luminaries is in an angle, and most of the attendant stars are either themselves in angles, or configurated with the angles., then Persons will not attain to any very eminent rank; yet they will take a leading part in ordinary civil and municipal affairs.
If The attendant stars have no configuration with the angles., then Persons will then remain altogether undistinguished and without advancement.
If Neither of the luminaries is found situated in a masculine sign, nor in an angle, nor is attended by any benefics., then Persons will be born to complete obscurity and adversity.
If The benefics, or stars of the same condition, exercise the chief dominion., then The dignities to be acquired will be not only important, but also more securely established.
If The malefics, or by stars of an adverse condition, claim the chief dominion., then The dignities will be more subordinate, and more dangerous and evanescent.
If Saturn have chief dominion of the doryphory., then The power and authority derived therefrom will lead to wealth and profit.
If Jupiter and Venus have chief dominion of the doryphory., then Authority proceeding from Jupiter and Venus will be pleasurable, and attended by presents and honours.
If Mars has chief dominion of the doryphory., then Authority proceeding from Mars will consist in commanding armies, in obtaining victories, and in overawing the vanquished.
If Mercury has chief dominion of the doryphory., then Authority proceeding from Mercury will be intellectual, superintending education and study, and directing the management of business.

If Mercury is the lord of the employment or profession., then Persons then born will become writers, superintendents of business, accountants, teachers in the sciences, merchants and bankers: also, soothsayers, astrologers, and attendants on sacrifices, and, in short, all who live by the exercise of literature, and by furnishing explanation or interpretation ; as well as by stipend and salary, or allowance.

If Saturn bear testimony jointly with Mercury., then Persons then born will become managers of the affairs of others, or interpreters of dreams, or will be engaged in temples for the purpose of divination, and for the sake of their fanaticism.
If Jupiter join testimony with Mercury., then Persons will be painters, orators, or pleaders in argument, and occupied with eminent personages.

If Venus has dominion of the employment., then She will cause persons to be engaged in the various perfumes of flowers, in unguents and wines, and also in colours, dyes, and in spices: thus she will produce vendors of unguents, garland-makers, wine-merchants, dealers in medical drugs, weavers, dealers in spices, painters, dyers, and vendors of apparel.

If Saturn add his testimony to Venus., then He will cause persons to be employed in matters belonging to amusement and decoration ; and will also produce jugglers, scorcerers and charlatans, and all such as practise similarly.
If Jupiter join testimony with Venus., then Persons will become prize-wrestlers, and garland-wearers, and will be advanced in honour through female interest.

If Mars, ruling the employment, and being configurated with the Sun., then Persons who operate by means of fire; for instance, cooks, as well as those who work in copper, brass, and other metals, by melting, burning, and casting.
If Mars be separated from the Sun., then He will make shipwrights, smiths, agriculturists, stonemasons, carpenters, and subordinate labourers.

If Saturn bear testimony, in addition to Mars., then Persons will decome mariners, workers in wells, vaults or mines, painters, keepers of beasts or cattle, cooks or butchers, and attendants on baths or on exhibitions.
If Jupiter join testimony with Mars., then They will be soldiers, or mechanics, collectors of revenue, inn-keepers, tollgatherers, or attendants on sacrifices.
If Two arbiters of employment may be found together, and provided they should be Mercury and Venus., then They will then produce musicians, melodists, and persons engaged in music, poetry, and songs.

If Two arbiters of employment may be found together, and provided they should be Mercury and Venus, especially if changed in their places., then They will also produce mimics, actors, dealers in slaves, makers of musical instruments, choristers and musical performers, dancers, weavers, modellers in wax, and painters.
If Saturn join testimony with Mercury and Venus., then The preparation and sale of female ornaments will be added to the aforesaid occupations.
If Mercury and Venus are lords of employment, then musicians, melodists, and persons engaged in music, poetry, and songs

If Mercury and Venus are lords of employment and changed in their places, then mimics, actors, dealers in slaves, makers of musical instruments, choristers and musical performers, dancers, weavers, modellers in wax, and painters
If Saturn joins testimony with Mercury and Venus as lords of employment, then preparation and sale of female ornaments will be added to the aforesaid occupations
If Jupiter gives testimony as lord of employment, then administrators of justice, guardians of public affairs, instructors of youth, and magistrates of the people

If Mercury and Mars together are lords of the employment, then statuaries, armour-makers, sculptors, modellers of animals, wrestlers, surgeons, spies or informers, adulterers, busy in crime, and forgers
If Saturn also bear testimony in addition to Mercury and Mars as lords of employment, then assassins, highwaymen, thieves, robbers lurking in ambush, marauders on cattle, and swindlers

If Jupiter afford testimony as lord of employment with Mercury and Mars, then honourable warfare, and in industry; making them cautious and diligent in business, curious in foreign matters, and deriving profit from their pursuits

If Venus and Mars exercise the dominion together, then dyers, dealers in unguents and perfumes, workers in tin, lead, gold, and silver, mock combatants or dancers in armour, dealers in medical drugs, agriculturists, and physicians, healing by means of medicine

If Saturn add testimony to Venus and Mars, then persons attendant on animals consecrated to religion; also grave-diggers and undertakers, mourners and musicians at funerals, and fanatics occupied in religious ceremonies, lamentations, and blood

If Jupiter add testimony to Venus and Mars, then regulators of sacrifices, augurs, holders of sacred offices, governors placed over women, and interpreters; and they will derive support from such occupations
If lords of the employment are posited in signs of human shape, then all scientific pursuits, and such as are of utility to mankind
If lords of the employment are posited in quadrupedal signs, then employment among metals, in business and trade, in housebuilding, and in the work of smiths and mechanics

If lords of the employment are posited in tropical and equinoctial signs, then employment in translation or interpretation, in matters of exchange, in mensuration and agriculture, and in religious duties
If lords of the employment are posited in terrestrial and watery signs, then employment in water, and in connection with water, as well in regard to the nurture of plants, as to ship-building
If lords of the employment are posited in terrestrial and watery signs, then employment in funerals, in embalming and preserving, and also in salt

If Moon herself actually occupy the place regulating the employment and after her conjunction, continue in course with Mercury, being at the same time in Taurus, Capricorn, or Cancer, then soothsayers, attendants on sacrifices, and ‘diviners by the basin

If Moon herself actually occupy the place regulating the employment and after her conjunction, continue in course with Mercury, being at the same time in Sagittarius or Pisces, then necromancers, and evokers of demons

If Moon herself actually occupy the place regulating the employment and after her conjunction, continue in course with Mercury, being at the same time in Virgo or Scorpio, then magicians, astrologers, and oracular persons, possessing prescience

If Moon herself actually occupy the place regulating the employment and after her conjunction, continue in course with Mercury, being at the same time in Libra, Aries, or Leo, then fanatics, interpreters of dreams, and makers of false vows and adjurations
If planets be oriental, or in angles, then give the person eminence and authority in his employment
If planets be occidental or cadent, then render him subordinate
If benefics be in elevation, then the employment will be important, lucrative, secure, honourable, and agreeable
If significators be in elevation above the lords of the employment, then it will then be mean, disreputable, unprofitable, and insecure
If Saturn brings an adverse influence, then coldness or tardiness, and in the composition or mixture of colours
If Mars produces opposition, then audacity and publicity in enterprise
If both Saturn and Mars, then alike hostile to proficiency and prosperity
If Moon be found in the oriental quadrants, then men either to marry early in life, or, after having over-passed their prime, to marry young women
If Moon be situated in either of the occidental quadrants, then men will then marry either late in life, or to women advanced in age
If Moon be found under the Sun’s beams, and configurated with Saturn, then she then entirely denies marriage
If Moon be in a sign of single form, and in application to only one of the planets, then she will cause men to marry only once
If Moon be in a bicorporeal or multiform sign, or in application to several planets, then she will cause them to be married several times
If planets, which thus, either by adjacency or by testimony, receive her application, be benefic, then men will then obtain good wives
If planets, which thus, either by adjacency or by testimony, receive her application, be malefic, then bad wives
If Saturn receive the Moon’s application, then the wives whom he will provide will be troublesome and morose
If Jupiter receive the Moon’s application, then they will be decorous and economical
If Mars receive the Moon’s application, then bold and refractory
If Venus receive the Moon’s application, then cheerful, handsome, and agreeable
If Mercury receive the Moon’s application, then sensible, prudent, and clever
If Venus be found connected with Jupiter, Saturn, or Mercury, then she will render wives provident, and attached to their husbands and children
If Venus be found connected with Mars, then they will be irascible, unsteady, and indiscreet
If Sun be posited in the oriental quadrants, then women will be married either in their own youth, or to men younger than themselves
If Sun be in the occidental quadrants, then they will either be married late in life, or to men who have passed their prime, and are advanced in years
If Sun be in a sign of single form, or configurated with only one oriental planet, then he will cause them to enter into matrimony only once
If Sun be in a bicorporeal or multiform sign, or configurated with several oriental planets, then he will then cause them to be often married
If Saturn, being configurated with the Sun, then will provide husbands steadfast, advantageous, and industrious
If Jupiter, being configurated with the Sun, then such as are honourable and noble-minded
If Mars, being configurated with the Sun, then severe husbands void of affection and intractable
If Venus, being configurated with the Sun, then amiable and handsome husbands
If Mercury, being configurated with the Sun, then such as are provident and expert in business
If Venus be found connected with Saturn, then she will indicate dull and timid husbands
If Venus be found connected with Jupiter, then the husbands will be good, just, and modest
If Venus be found connected with Mars, then hasty, lustful, and adulterous
If Venus be found connected with Mercury, then they will be extravagantly desirous of young persons

If both nativities, viz. that of the husband and that of the wife, may exhibit the luminaries configurated together in concord, that Is to say, either in trine or in sextile to each other, then the cohabitation will most usually be lasting
If the said concord exist by means of interchange, then its duration will be also much more securely established
If the Moon in the husband’s nativity should correspond or agree with the Sun, then its duration will be also much more securely established
If Luminaries in husband and wife's nativities are in trine or sextile, then Cohabitation will be lasting
If Moon in husband's nativity corresponds with Sun in wife's nativity, then Duration of cohabitation will be securely established
If Luminaries are in signs inconjunct, in opposition, or in quartile, then Cohabitation will be speedily dissolved upon slight causes, and the total separation of the parties will ensue
If Configuration of luminaries in concord is aspected by benefics, then Cohabitation will continue in respectability, comfort, and advantage
If Configuration of luminaries in concord is aspected by malefics, then Cohabitation will abound in strife, contention, and misfortune

If Benefics offer testimony to luminaries not favorably configured in concord, then Cohabitation will not be entirely broken off, nor totally destroyed for ever, but will be again renewed, and re-established as before
If Malefics bear testimony to discordant disposition of the luminaries, then Dissolution of the cohabitation will take place, accompanied by scorn and injury
If Mercury alone is conjoined with the malefics, then Dissolution of cohabitation will be effected by means of some public inculpation
If Venus is found with malefics, then Dissolution of cohabitation will be on the ground of adultery, or sorcery, or some similar offence
If Venus, Mars, and Saturn act in familiarity with the luminaries, then Cohabitation will be appropriate and domestic, and authorised by law
If Venus is in concurrence with Mars, then Entire love and affection in the cohabiting parties
If Mercury also coincide with Venus and Mars, then Such affection will become publicly notorious
If Venus is found in a sign mutually common and familiar, such as Capricorn, or Pisces, then Marriages between brothers and sisters and kindred by blood
If Venus is in the presence of the Moon, when the native may be male, then He will cause him to connect himself with two sisters, or other near relatives
If Venus is with Jupiter, when the native be a female, then A similar contract on her part, with two brothers or near relatives, will be indicated
If Venus is with Saturn, then Cohabitation will be established entirely in happiness and constancy
If Mercury is present with Venus and Saturn, then Cohabitation will be profitable
If Mars is present with Venus and Saturn, then Cohabitation will be unsettled, calamitous, and afflicted by jealousy
If Mars is configurated on equal terms with Venus, Saturn, and Mercury, then Marriage between persons of equal age
If Mars is more oriental, then Marriage will take place with a younger man or woman
If Mars is more occidental, then Marriage will take place with an older person
If Venus and Saturn are found in signs common to each other, that is to say, in Capricorn and Libra, then Marriage will be contracted between persons kindred by blood

If Venus and Saturn are found in signs common to each other, that is to say, in Capricorn and Libra in the ascendant, or in the mid-heaven, and the Moon also should present herself there, then Men will become connected with their mothers, or maternal aunts, or with their mothers-in-law; and women with their own sons, or the sons of their brothers, or with their daughters’ husbands

If Venus and Saturn are found in signs common to each other, that is to say, in Capricorn and Libra in the ascendant, or in the mid-heaven, and the Sun also should present herself there, and especially should it happen that the planets in question may be occidental, then Men will then connect thenselves with their daughters, or the wives of their sons ; and women with their fathers, or paternal uncles, or the husbands of their daughters
If Venus and Saturn are in feminine places, then They will render the parties obscene, lustful and shameless
If Venus and Saturn are posited in angles, the eastern and southern, then Produce a total exposure of the passions, and cause them to be publicly canvassed
If Venus and Saturn are posited in angles, the western and northern, then Produce eunuchs, or persons unprolific, and not possessing the proper channels of nature

If Mars is separated from Venus and Saturn, but yet, at the same time, be supported by the testimony of Jupiter, then He will make men pure and decorous in sexual intercourse, and incline them to natural usages only
If Mars attach himself to Saturn only, then He will render them cold in blood and dull in appetite

If Saturn and Mars may be thus connected together, Venus and Jupiter should also be configurated with them, then Men will then become easily excited and eager in desire, although they will still be continent, and restrain themselves in order to avoid reproach
If Saturn be absent, and Mars be with Venus alone, or even although Jupiter also be with her, then Men will become highly licentious, and attempt to gratify their desires in every mode
If Venus be found more occidental, then Men will connect themselves with low women, female servants, and aliens or vagabonds
If Mars be found occidental, then With women of rank, and gentlewomen; or with women living with their husbands, or under the protection of men
If Venus be configurated with Jupiter, or with Mercury, then She will cause women to be temperate and pure in sexual intercourse
If Venus be thus connected with Mercury, if Saturn be not present also, then She will cause them to be easily excited to desire; although they will control their desires, and avoid reproach
If Venus be conjoined or configurated with Mars alone, then She will render women licentious and lustful

If To Venus and Mars, when thus conjoined or configurated, Jupiter also present himself, Mars being at the same time under the rays of the Sun, then Women will then mingle in intercourse with servants, and persons meaner than themselves, or with aliens, or vagabonds
If Venus may be under the rays of the Sun, then They will then connect themselves with their superiors or masters
If The planets be in feminine places, or configurated femininely, then They will be content with their passive faculties only
If Saturn, in being conciliated with such positions as those now described, then Tends to produce greater obscenity
If Jupiter, in being conciliated with such positions as those now described, then Greater decency
If Mercury, in being conciliated with such positions as those now described, then Greater publicity, and greater fickleness, or instability
If Moon, Jupiter, and Venus are posited in, or configurated with the place on the zenith, or its succedent house, then Givers of offspring
If Sun, Mars, and Saturn are posited in, or configurated with the place on the zenith, or its succedent house, then Denying children altogether, or as allowing but few
If Moon, Jupiter, and Venus are in the zenith or its succedent house, or configurated with them, then givers of offspring
If Sun, Mars, and Saturn are in the zenith or its succedent house, or configurated with them, then denying children altogether, or as allowing but few
If Mercury is oriental, then gives offspring
If Mercury is occidental, then withholds offspring
If planets which grant progeny are posited singly, then the gift of progeny will be single only
If planets which grant progeny are in bicorporeal or in feminine signs, then they will grant double offsprings
If planets which grant progeny are in prolific or seminal signs, such as Pisces, Cancer, and Scorpio, then they will grant twins, or even more
If planets which grant progeny are masculinely constituted by configuration with the Sun, as by being in masculine signs, then they will grant male children
If planets which grant progeny are femininely constituted, then they will grant female children
If planets which grant progeny are beneath the malefics in elevation, or in barren places, or in signs such as those of Leo and Virgo, then children will neither be healthy, nor continue in life

If Sun and the malefics are in entire possession of the places above mentioned, viz. that on the zenith, or the succedent house allotted to the good demon, and in masculine or barren signs, and the benefics are not in elevation above them, then a total privation of offspring is thereby indicated

If Sun and the malefics are in entire possession of the places above mentioned, viz. that on the zenith, or the succedent house allotted to the good demon, and in feminine or prolific signs, or supported by the testimony of the benefics, then children will then be granted; yet they will be liable to disease, and short-lived

If planets of each condition are configurated with, and-have prerogative in prolific signs, then there will then ensue a loss of either all the children, or only few, or else the major part of them ; in the same proportion as that in which the planets, bearing testimony to either condition, may preponderate on one side rather than the other ; by excelling in number, or in influence, in consequence of being posited more orientally, more genuinely in angles, higher in elevation, or successively ascending
If lords of the aforesaid signs are givers of offspring, and are either oriental, or in places proper to themselves, then the children thus granted will become eminent and illustrious
If lords of the aforesaid signs are givers of offspring, and are occidental, or in places not proper to themselves, then the children will then become undistinguished and abject

If lords of the aforesaid signs are in concord with the part of fortune, and with the ascendant, then they will render the children amiable, and cause them to be beloved by their parents, and to inherit their parents’ substance

If lords of the aforesaid signs are inconjunct, and not in concord with the part of fortune, and with the ascendant, then the children will then become odious and mischievous to their parents, and will forfeit the inheritance of their substance
If planets which grant progeny are appropriately configurated with each other, then they will promote brotherly love, and | mutual regard and affection among the children
If planets which grant progeny are inconjunct, or in opposition, then they will excite in them mutual hatred, deceit
If Sun, Moon, ascendant, and part of fortune in both nativities are in the same signs, then they will create fixed and indissoluble friendship

If Sun, Moon, ascendant, and part of fortune in both nativities are counterchanged in position in each nativity, and especially should the two ascendants be within the distance of seventeen degrees from each other, then they will create fixed and indissoluble friendship
If Sun, Moon, ascendant, and part of fortune in both nativities are in signs inconjunct, or in opposition, then they will produce great and lasting enmity

If Sun, Moon, ascendant, and part of fortune in one nativity be configurated with such parts of the zodiac as are occupied by the Sun, &c., in the other nativity by trine or sextile, then they will then produce minor friendship

If Sun, Moon, ascendant, and part of fortune in one nativity be configurated with such parts of the zodiac as are occupied by the Sun, &c., in the other nativity by quartile, then they will excite minor enmity, so as to take effect at certain particular times, in which the friendship remains, as it were, inactive and subdued, while the malefics transit the configuration
If all or most of the aforesaid places be in familiarity with each other, then friendship of all the three kinds will be established
If the places be entirely without familiarity, then similar enmity will be established
If familiarity, or absence of familiarity (as the case may be), exist only as regards the places of the luminaries, then friendship or enmity will then be established by spontaneous will
If friendship is produced by spontaneous will, then the best and most secure
If enmity is produced by spontaneous will, then the worst and most dangerous
If friendship, or enmity, consequent on the familiarity or non-familiarity of the respective parts of Fortune, then will be established on the idea of profit
If friendship, or enmity, consequent on a similar disposition of the respective ascendants, then will arise from pain or pleasure mutually excited between the parties
If Saturn and Jupiter, when making ingress upon each other’s places, then produce friendship by certain agreements, or engagements, relating either to agriculture or to inheritance
If Saturn and Mars, then create contention and treachery spontaneously entertained
If Saturn and Venus, then friendship between kindred ; liable, however, soon to grow cool
If Saturn and Mercury, then friendship on account of business, or profit, or some secret art or mystery
If Jupiter and Mars, then create friendship in the direction of affairs, and by means of dignities
If Jupiter and Venus, then create friendship by means of female persons, or attendants on religion, or on oracles
If Jupiter and Mercury, then friendship by means of eloquence and science, and philosophical inclinations
If Mars and Venus, then cause friendship in the course of amours, adultery, — and fornication
If Mars and Mercury, then excite hatred and strife by offences committed in business and trade, or by sorcery
If Venus and Mercury, then produce communion by means of the arts and sciences, by a mutual interest in literature, or by female persons
If Mars and Venus, then friendship in the course of amours, adultery, and fornication
If Mars and Mercury, then hatred and strife by offences committed in business and trade, or by sorcery
If Venus and Mercury, then communion by means of the arts and sciences, by a mutual interest in literature, or by female persons
If Moon descending or cadent from the angles, then journeys and changes of residence
If Mars descending or cadent from the zenith, in quartile or opposition to the luminaries, then journeys and changes of residence
If Part of Fortune placed in signs which produce travelling, then the course and practice of the whole life will be engaged in foreign lands
If Benefics superintend the aforesaid places, or ascend in succession to them, then the engagements abroad will be honourable and lucrative, and the return home speedy and unobstructed
If Malefics superintend or ascend in succession to those places, then the journey outward will then lead to peril and misfortune, and the return will be replete with difficulty
If Luminaries posited in the cadent houses of the oriental quadrants, then travel will take place in the eastern or southern quarters of the world
If Luminaries placed in western situations, or in an occidental quadrant, then travel will be then prosecuted in the northern or western parts

If Signs, which operate travel, be themselves single in form, or should the planets, having dominion of them, be singly posited, then the journeys will then take place after long intervals, and occasionally only
If Signs be bicorporeal, or double in form or figure, then travel will be constantly repeated and continued

If Jupiter and Venus may be in dominion over the luminaries, and over the places producing travel, then the journeys agreeable, as well as free from danger: for the traveller will be joyfully forwarded on his way by the magistrates of the country, and by the concurrent assistance of friendly persons ; the state of the atmosphere will also be favourable, and he will meet with abundance of accommodation
If Mercury also be present with Jupiter and Venus, then utility, profit, presents and honours will likewise be derived from the journey
If Saturn and Mars, if controlling the luminaries, and, especially, if placed distant from each other, then great dangers, and at the same time render the journey fruitless and unavailing
If Saturn and Mars in watery signs, then the dangers will arise by shipwreck, or among deserts and wilderness
If Saturn and Mars in fixed signs, then by precipices, and adverse blasts of wind
If Saturn and Mars in tropical and equinoctial signs, then by want of food and other necessaries, and by some unwholesome state of the atmosphere
If Saturn and Mars in signs of human form, then by robbery, treachery, and various depredations
If Saturn and Mars in terrestrial signs, then by the attack of wild beasts, or from earthquakes
If Mercury also lend concurrence to Saturn and Mars, then the traveller will incur further danger from accusations made against him, as well as from reptiles and venomous stings or bites

If Lords of death fully possess their own peculiar and natural properties, and neither of the malefics may be in elevation above them, then death will ensue in the modes above detailed, and in the ordinary course of nature
If Both malefics, either in conjunction, or in quartile or Opposition to each other, may be lords of the anzretic places, then a violent and remarkable death will occur
If Both or one of the malefics should attack either both the luminaries, or even only the Sun or the Moon, then a violent and remarkable death will occur
If Saturn in fixed signs, and in quartile or opposition to the Sun, and contrary in condition, then death by suffocation, occasioned either by multitudes of people, or by hanging or strangulation
If Saturn occidental, and the Moon succedent to him, then death by suffocation, occasioned either by multitudes of people, or by hanging or strangulation
If Saturn be posited in places or signs of bestial form, then the native will be destroyed by wild beasts

If Jupiter also offer testimony, being at the same time badly afflicted, with Saturn in places or signs of bestial form, then the death will then occur in public, and by day; for example, by being exposed to combats with wild beasts
If Saturn be posited in opposition to either of the luminaries in the ascendant, then death in prison

If Saturn be configurated with Mercury, and especially if near the constellation of the Serpent in the sphere, and in terrestrial signs of the zodiac, then death by venomous wounds or bites, and by reptiles and wild beasts
If Venus also attach herself to Saturn and Mercury thus combined, then death will then ensue by poison or female treachery
If Saturn be in Virgo or Pisces, or watery signs, and configurated with the Moon, then death by means of water, by drowning and suffocation; and, if found near Argo, by shipwreck
If Saturn be in tropical or quadrupedal signs, and the Sun be either in conjunction with him, or in opposition, then death will be caused by the fall of houses or buildings
If Saturn be in tropical or quadrupedal signs, and Mars be either in conjunction with him, or in opposition, then death will be caused by the fall of houses or buildings
If Saturn posited in the mid-heaven, then death will happen by falls from heights or precipices
If Mars, if in signs of human form, and posited in quartile or in opposition to the Sun or Moon, and contrary in condition, then death by slaughter, either in civil or foreign war
If Saturn with the Sun, or in opposition, then death
If Mars with the Sun, or in opposition, then death caused by the fall of houses or buildings
If Mars posited in the mid-heaven, then death will happen by falls from heights or precipices
If Mars in signs of human form, and posited in quartile or in opposition to the Sun or Moon, and contrary in condition, then death by slaughter, either in civil or foreign war, or by suicide

If Venus adds her testimony to Mars in signs of human form, and posited in quartile or in opposition to the Sun or Moon, and contrary in condition, then death will be inflicted by women, or by assassins in the employment of women

If Mercury also is configurated with Mars in signs of human form, and posited in quartile or in opposition to the Sun or Moon, and contrary in condition, then death will happen from robbers, thieves, or highwaymen
If Mars be in mutilated or imperfect signs, or near the Gorgon of Perseus, then death by decapitation, or by mutilation of limb
If Mars found in Scorpio or Taurus, then death by surgical amputation, burning or searing, or also by spasms or convulsions
If Mars found in the mid-heaven, either above or below the earth, especially if he be in the vicinity of Cepheus or Andromeda, then death will be inflicted by crucifixion or impalement
If Mars descending, or in opposition to the ascendant, then death by fire
If Mars in quadrupedal signs, then death by falls and fractures
If Jupiter bear testimony to Mars, and be at the same time afflicted, then death will ensue from the wrath of princes and kings, and from judicial condemnation

If the malefics be in concurrence with each other in the first instance, and afterwards in mutual opposition, in any of the aforesaid situations, then the evil character of the death will be yet further augmented
If both the malefics claim prerogative in the aneretic places, then the bodies of persons who thus die will be cast abroad without interment, and will be devoured by beasts and birds

If the malefics may be found in signs similar in form to beasts and birds; and provided not any one of the benefics should offer testimony to the place below the earth, nor to the aneretic places, then the bodies of persons who thus die will be cast abroad without interment, and will be devoured by beasts and birds
If the planets controlling the aneretic places may be posited in cadent houses, then death will occur in foreign lands

If the planets controlling the aneretic places may be posited in cadent houses, especially if the Moon be present in the said places also, or if she be found in quartile or in opposition, then death will occur in foreign lands
If Prorogation from the ascendant, then Events affecting the body, travelling, or change of residence
If Prorogation from the part of Fortune, then Incidents affecting substance or wealth
If Prorogation from the Moon, then Actions of the mind, communion, and cohabitation
If Prorogation from the Sun, then Dignities and glory
If Prorogation from the mid-heaven, then Employment, friendship, and the possession of children
If Meetings of all the benefics in all or most of the prorogations, then Perfect good fortune
If Meetings of all the malefics in all or most of the prorogations, then Distress
If Sun and Moon in concord with operative places at the ingresses, then Good
If Sun and Moon adversely posited, then Evil
If Sun and Moon not in concord with the said places, contrary in condition, and in opposition or in quartile, to the transits, then Evil
If Same planets are lords of the times and of the ingresses, of a favorable nature, then Effect will be extreme and unalloyed
If Same planets are lords of the times and of the ingresses, of an evil nature, then Effect will be more particularly unmitigated

If Planets are lords of the times and hold dominion from the date of the nativity, and all or most of the prorogations tend to, or depend on, one and the same place, then Wholly produce, in all respects, good or evil fortune
If Prorogations are not so constituted, yet the meetings occurring at the periods are found to be either all, or most of them, benefic, then Wholly produce, in all respects, good fortune
If Prorogations are not so constituted, yet the meetings occurring at the periods are found to be either all, or most of them, malefic, then Wholly produce, in all respects, evil fortune

If Planets are lords of the times and hold dominion from the date of the nativity, and prorogations tend to or depend on one place, then Good or evil fortune will be produced respectively based on whether the meetings occurring at the periods are benefic or malefic.

If Planets are lords of the times and hold dominion from the date of the nativity, and prorogations are not so constituted, but the meetings occurring at the periods are benefic or malefic, then Good or evil fortune will be produced respectively based on whether the meetings occurring at the periods are benefic or malefic.
If Fixed stars and planets are posited on the same circles drawn through the poles of the zodiac, then Aspects are properly considered as made to the planets by the fixed stars.

If Fixed stars and planets are posited on different circles, but a trinal or sextile distance is preserved between them, and the fixed stars are on parts of the circle liable to be transited by any of the planets, then Aspects are properly considered as made to the planets by the fixed stars.
If A star is found together with the Sun in the oriental horizon, then Matutine subsolar aspect
If A star rises immediately after the Sun, then Oriental, invisible, and succedent rising aspect
If A star is found in partile conjunction with the Sun in the oriental horizon, then Precise oriental co-rising aspect
If A star rises before the Sun, then Oriental, precedent, and visible rising aspect
If A star is found on the meridian, either above or below the earth, while the Sun is on the oriental horizon, then Matutine location in the mid-heaven aspect
If Immediately after the Sun’s rising, the star shall be found on the meridian, then Succedent and oriental location in the mid-heaven, invisible aspect
If Exactly as the Sun rises, the star is at the same time on the meridian, then Precise oriental location in the mid-heaven aspect
If The star first shall come to the meridian above the earth, and the Sun may then immediately rise, then Oriental precedent location in the mid-heaven aspect
If The Sun may be actually in the oriental horizon, but the star in the occidental, then Matutine setting aspect
If The star sets immediately after the Sun’s rising, then Oriental, succedent setting, invisible aspect
If The star sets at the moment of the Sun’s rising, then Precise oriental co-setting aspect
If Sun is on the meridian above the earth, and a star rises, then Diurnal and invisible aspect
If Sun is on the meridian below the earth, and a star rises, then Nocturnal and visible aspect
If Star and Sun are on the meridian above the earth, then Diurnal and invisible aspect
If Star is diametrically opposite to the Sun on the meridian below the earth, then Diurnal and invisible aspect
If Star and Sun are on the meridian under the earth, then Nocturnal and invisible aspect
If Star is diametrically opposite to the Sun on the meridian above the earth, then Nocturnal and visible aspect
If Star sets while the Sun is above the earth on the meridian, then Diurnal and invisible aspect
If Star sets while the Sun is on the meridian below the earth, then Nocturnal and visible aspect
If Star rises immediately after sunset, then Vespertine succedent rising, visible
If Star rises and the Sun sets at the same time, then Precise vespertine co-rising
If Star rises immediately before the Sun sets, then Precedent, vespertine rising, invisible
If Star is on the meridian immediately after sunset, then Visible vespertine location in the mid-heaven
If Star is on the meridian at the moment of sunset, then Precise vespertine location in the mid-heaven
If Star arrives on the meridian immediately before sunset, then Vespertine precedent location in the mid-heaven, invisible
If Star sets immediately after the Sun, then Vespertine, succedent and visible setting
If Star sets at the same moment with the Sun, then Precise vespertine setting
If Star sets before the Sun, then Precedent, invisible setting
If Seventh house and its lord afflicted, then Astrologer will be entangled in a labyrinth of error
If Benefics controlled in the eighth house, then They bring mischief by means of good men
If Benefics well affected in the eighth house, then They will prevent mischief
If A benefic ascends and both luminaries are in the same minute, then The native will be equally and highly prosperous in all things which can befall him
If The luminaries are mutually opposed by the east and west, then The native will be equally and highly prosperous in all things which can befall him
If A malefic is on the ascendant, then The contrary effect will be produced
If Moon's conjunction with Jupiter, then The efficacy of purgation is impeded
If Moon in Scorpio, then Purgation may be advantageously used, provided the lord of the ascendant be coupled with some star posited below the earth
If Moon in Pisces, then Purgation may be advantageously used, provided the lord of the ascendant be coupled with some star posited below the earth
If Lord of the ascendant coupled with a star placed above the earth when the Moon is in Scorpio or Pisces, then The potion swallowed will be vomited up
If Moon located in Leo, then Neither put on nor lay aside any garment for the first time
If Moon badly affected in Leo, then Neither put on nor lay aside any garment for the first time
If Aspects between the Moon and stars, then Give the native much activity
If Stars in power aspecting the Moon, then Indicate an efficient excitation to action
If Stars weak aspecting the Moon, then Indicate an inert excitation to action
If Eclipse of the luminaries in the angles of the nativity, then Is noxious
If Eclipse of the luminaries in the angles of an annual revolution, then Is noxious
